You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.185 (commit: bbfd8f4a). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: SVG CSS update via JS incorrect rendering. ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? I have an svg file, and a simple template that updates the SVG style using Javascript. ```xml ``` ```html

Key?

{{English}}

``` ### Expected behaviour Anki Desktop: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/7fc5ac58-33b3-4871-a52b-627b1e7bf4b7) ### Actual behaviour But the CSS modifications from JS don't work on AnkiDroid: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/e53a1b3a-426b-43b9-b0f1-cae7f5b30dd7) ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 13 (SDK 33) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = Google Model = Pixel 4a Hardware = sunfish Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; Pixel 4a Build/TQ3A.230805.001.S1; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.64 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = c023d340-ad1e-45d7-bbe3-fab43e49f220 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? Not sure where to start with debugging this. Pretty new to JS coding, and I don't see anything obvious here that would break the script. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Hello! 👋 Thanks for logging this issue. Please remember we are all volunteers here, so some patience may be required before we can get to the issue. Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing ``` "Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'querySelectorAll')" ``` Also didn't work in 2.16. Probably it's [same-origin policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Same-origin_policy) denying `.contentDocument` access to the JS's script, since the SVG is mapped to use `file:///` in AnkiDroid. Maybe [CORS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS) can be used or another approach (I'm not proefficient in that) The options I see: ### 1. Disable CORS That needs to manually serve files either in `Webview.shouldInterceptRequest` or `NanoHTTPD.serve` Downside: loading media is way slower than using `file:/` protocol ### 2. Change the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL Before, the media directory was set as the `baseUrl` of the reviewer and files were automatically served with `file:/` protocol with top-notch performance and no CORS issues. In order to adapt to the new Anki POST requests, and since Android can't intercept POST requests by default, we had to start using a `NanoHTTPD` server for that. Also for that, the reviewer's webview uses now `http://127.0.0.1:listeningPort` which may have CORS issues By changing the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL, both problems would be solved. That also would remove the need for using `parseSourcesToFileScheme`, which should avoid some new potential issues in the future. I believe that that can be done by adding some kind of `postRequestsBaseUrl` property to the backend and use it at the post method: https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/f73eb01047cfba28a4f29ccc3d898b2ca8c9d95b/ts/lib/post.ts#L9-L26 Is something like that possible, @dae? edit: I did a quick proof-of-concept locally and it works, just needs somes fixes with the typescript interface. Won't be able to do that for the time being ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/RangeHeader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/CardViewerFragment.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.
I'll analyze this issue and implement a fix. Let me start by understanding the current implementation by examining the relevant files.

16→package com.ichi2.anki
17→
18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
20→import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
21→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
22→import timber.log.Timber
23→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
24→import java.io.File
25→import java.io.FileInputStream
26→
27→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
28→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
29→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
30→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
31→        .setDomain(domain)
32→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
33→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
34→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
35→            }
36→            try {
37→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()
46→}

17→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
18→
19→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD.IHTTPSession
20→import java.io.File
21→import java.io.InputStream
22→import java.io.RandomAccessFile
23→import java.nio.channels.Channels
24→
25→/**
26→ * Handles the "range" header in a HTTP Request
27→ */
28→data class RangeHeader(val start: Long, val end: Long) {
29→    val contentLength: Long get() = end - start + 1
30→
31→    companion object {
32→        fun from(session: IHTTPSession, defaultEnd: Long): RangeHeader? {
33→            val range = session.headers["range"]?.trim() ?: return null
34→            val numbers = range.substring("bytes=".length).split('-')
35→            val unspecifiedEnd = numbers.getOrNull(1).isNullOrEmpty()
36→            return RangeHeader(
37→                start = numbers[0].toLong(),
38→                end = if (unspecifiedEnd) defaultEnd else numbers[1].toLong()
39→            )
40→        }
41→    }
42→}
43→
44→fun File.toInputStream(header: RangeHeader): InputStream {
45→    // PERF: Test to see if a custom FileInputStream + available() would be faster
46→    val randomAccessFile = RandomAccessFile(this, "r")
47→    return Channels.newInputStream(randomAccessFile.channel).also {
48→        it.skip(header.start)
49→    }
50→}
51→

 15→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 16→ */
 17→
 18→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19→
 20→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→
 26→const val PORT = 0
 27→// const val PORT = 40001
 28→
 29→// local debugging:
 30→// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31→
 32→open class AnkiServer(
 33→    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34→) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35→
 36→    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37→        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41→    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42→    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43→
 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        return when (session.method) {
 46→            Method.POST -> {
 47→                val uri = session.uri
 48→                Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 49→                val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 50→                buildResponse {
 51→                    postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 52→                }
 53→            }
 54→            Method.GET -> newFixedLengthResponse(Response.Status.NOT_FOUND, null, null)
 55→            else -> newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 56→        }
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun buildResponse(
 60→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 61→    ): Response {
 62→        return try {
 63→            val data = runBlocking {
 64→                block()
 65→            }
 66→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 67→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 68→            Timber.w(exc, "buildResponse failure")
 69→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 70→        }
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    companion object {
 74→        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 75→
 76→        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 77→        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 78→        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 79→
 80→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 81→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 82→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 83→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 84→            return bytes
 85→        }
 86→
 87→        fun newChunkedResponse(
 88→            data: ByteArray?,
 89→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
 90→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
 91→        ): Response {
 92→            return if (data == null) {
 93→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 94→            } else {
 95→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
 96→            }
 97→        }
 98→    }
 99→}
100→

16→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
17→
18→import android.os.Bundle
19→import android.view.View
20→import android.webkit.WebChromeClient
21→import android.webkit.WebView
22→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
23→import androidx.annotation.CallSuper
24→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
25→import com.google.android.material.appbar.MaterialToolbar
26→import com.ichi2.anki.R
27→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
28→import timber.log.Timber
29→
30→/**
31→ * Base class for displaying Anki HTML pages
32→ */
33→@Suppress("LeakingThis")
34→abstract class PageFragment : Fragment(R.layout.page_fragment), PostRequestHandler {
35→    abstract val title: String
36→    abstract val pageName: String
37→
38→    lateinit var webView: WebView
39→    private val server = AnkiServer(this).also { it.start() }
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Override this to set a custom [WebViewClient] to the page.
43→     * This is called in [onViewCreated].
44→     *
45→     * @param savedInstanceState If non-null, this fragment is being re-constructed
46→     * from a previous saved state as given here.
47→     */
48→    protected open fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) = PageWebViewClient()
49→
50→    /**
51→     * Override this to set a custom [WebChromeClient] to the page.
52→     * This is called in [onViewCreated].
53→     *
54→     * @param savedInstanceState If non-null, this fragment is being re-constructed
55→     * from a previous saved state as given here.
56→     */
57→    protected open fun onCreateWebChromeClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) = PageChromeClient()
58→
59→    @CallSuper
60→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
61→        webView = view.findViewById<WebView>(R.id.webview).apply {
62→            with(settings) {
63→                javaScriptEnabled = true
64→                displayZoomControls = false
65→                builtInZoomControls = true
66→                setSupportZoom(true)
67→            }
68→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
69→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient(savedInstanceState)
70→        }
71→        val nightMode = if (Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode) "#night" else ""
72→        val url = server.baseUrl() + "backend/web/$pageName.html$nightMode"
73→
74→        Timber.i("Loading $url")
75→        webView.loadUrl(url)
76→
77→        view.findViewById<MaterialToolbar>(R.id.toolbar).apply {
78→            title = this@PageFragment.title
79→            setNavigationOnClickListener {
80→                requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
81→            }
82→        }
83→    }
84→
85→    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
86→        val methodName = if (uri.startsWith(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX)) {
87→            uri.substring(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX.length)
88→        } else {
89→            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
90→        }
91→        return activity.handleUiPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
92→            ?: handleCollectionPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
93→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled method: $methodName")
94→    }
95→}
96→

 17→package com.ichi2.anki
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.database.sqlite.SQLiteFullException
 22→import android.os.Environment
 23→import android.text.format.Formatter
 24→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 25→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 26→import androidx.core.content.edit
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidFolder.AppPrivateFolder
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.exception.StorageAccessException
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.exception.UnknownDatabaseVersionException
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.Preferences
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 32→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 33→import com.ichi2.libanki.DB
 34→import com.ichi2.preferences.getOrSetString
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.FileUtil
 36→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 37→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.BackendException.BackendDbException.BackendDbFileTooNewException
 38→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.BackendException.BackendDbException.BackendDbLockedException
 39→import timber.log.Timber
 40→import java.io.File
 41→import java.io.FileNotFoundException
 42→import java.io.IOException
 43→import java.lang.Exception
 44→import kotlin.Throws
 45→
 46→/**
 47→ * Singleton which opens, stores, and closes the reference to the Collection.
 48→ */
 49→@KotlinCleanup("convert to object")
 50→open class CollectionHelper {
 51→    /**
 52→     * Get the single instance of the [Collection], creating it if necessary  (lazy initialization).
 53→     * @param context is no longer used, as the global AnkidroidApp instance is used instead
 54→     * @return instance of the Collection
 55→     */
 56→    @Synchronized
 57→    open fun getColUnsafe(context: Context?): Collection? {
 58→        return CollectionManager.getColUnsafe()
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    /**
 62→     * Calls [getColUnsafe] inside a try / catch statement.
 63→     * Send exception report if [reportException] is set and return null if there was an exception.
 64→     * @param context
 65→     * @param reportException Whether to send a crash report if an [Exception] was thrown when opening the collection (excluding
 66→     * [BackendDbLockedException] and [BackendDbFileTooNewException]).
 67→     * @return the [Collection] if it could be obtained, `null` otherwise.
 68→     */
 69→    @Synchronized
 70→    fun tryGetColUnsafe(context: Context?, reportException: Boolean = true): Collection? {
 71→        lastOpenFailure = null
 72→        return try {
 73→            getColUnsafe(context)
 74→        } catch (e: BackendDbLockedException) {
 75→            lastOpenFailure = CollectionOpenFailure.LOCKED
 76→            Timber.w(e)
 77→            null
 78→        } catch (e: BackendDbFileTooNewException) {
 79→            lastOpenFailure = CollectionOpenFailure.FILE_TOO_NEW
 80→            Timber.w(e)
 81→            null
 82→        } catch (e: SQLiteFullException) {
 83→            lastOpenFailure = CollectionOpenFailure.DISK_FULL
 84→            Timber.w(e)
 85→            null
 86→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 87→            lastOpenFailure = CollectionOpenFailure.CORRUPT
 88→            Timber.w(e)
 89→            if (reportException) {
 90→                CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport(e, "CollectionHelper.getColSafe")
 91→            }
 92→            null
 93→        }
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    /**
 97→     * Close the [Collection], optionally saving
 98→     * @param save whether or not save before closing
 99→     */
100→    @Synchronized
101→    fun closeCollection(reason: String?) {
102→        Timber.i("closeCollection: %s", reason)
103→        CollectionManager.closeCollectionBlocking()
104→    }
105→
106→    /**
107→     * @return Whether or not [Collection] and its child database are open.
108→     */
109→    fun colIsOpenUnsafe(): Boolean {
110→        return CollectionManager.isOpenUnsafe()
111→    }
112→
113→    /**
114→     * This currently stores either:
115→     * An error message stating the reason that a storage check must be performed
116→     * OR
117→     * The current storage requirements, and the current available storage.
118→     */
119→    @KotlinCleanup("extract this to another file")
120→    class CollectionIntegrityStorageCheck {
121→        private val errorMessage: String?
122→
123→        // OR:
124→        private val requiredSpace: Long?
125→        private val freeSpace: Long?
126→
127→        private constructor(requiredSpace: Long, freeSpace: Long) {
128→            this.freeSpace = freeSpace
129→            this.requiredSpace = requiredSpace
130→            errorMessage = null
131→        }
132→
133→        private constructor(errorMessage: String) {
134→            requiredSpace = null
135→            freeSpace = null
136→            this.errorMessage = errorMessage
137→        }
138→
139→        fun shouldWarnOnIntegrityCheck(): Boolean {
140→            return errorMessage != null || fileSystemDoesNotHaveSpaceForBackup()
141→        }
142→
143→        private fun fileSystemDoesNotHaveSpaceForBackup(): Boolean {
144→            // only to be called when mErrorMessage == null
145→            if (freeSpace == null || requiredSpace == null) {
146→                Timber.e("fileSystemDoesNotHaveSpaceForBackup called in invalid state.")
147→                return true
148→            }
149→            Timber.d("Required Free Space: %d. Current: %d", requiredSpace, freeSpace)
150→            return requiredSpace > freeSpace
151→        }
152→
153→        fun getWarningDetails(context: Context): String {
154→            if (errorMessage != null) {
155→                return errorMessage
156→            }
157→            if (freeSpace == null || requiredSpace == null) {
158→                Timber.e("CollectionIntegrityCheckStatus in an invalid state")
159→                val defaultRequiredFreeSpace = defaultRequiredFreeSpace(context)
160→                return context.resources.getString(
161→                    R.string.integrity_check_insufficient_space,
162→                    defaultRequiredFreeSpace
163→                )
164→            }
165→            val required = Formatter.formatShortFileSize(context, requiredSpace)
166→            val insufficientSpace = context.resources.getString(
167→                R.string.integrity_check_insufficient_space,
168→                required
169→            )
170→
171→            // Also concat in the extra content showing the current free space.
172→            val currentFree = Formatter.formatShortFileSize(context, freeSpace)
173→            val insufficientSpaceCurrentFree = context.resources.getString(
174→                R.string.integrity_check_insufficient_space_extra_content,
175→                currentFree
176→            )
177→            return insufficientSpace + insufficientSpaceCurrentFree
178→        }
179→
180→        companion object {
181→
182→            private fun fromError(errorMessage: String): CollectionIntegrityStorageCheck {
183→                return CollectionIntegrityStorageCheck(errorMessage)
184→            }
185→
186→            private fun defaultRequiredFreeSpace(context: Context): String {
187→                val oneHundredFiftyMB =
188→                    (150 * 1000 * 1000).toLong() // tested, 1024 displays 157MB. 1000 displays 150
189→                return Formatter.formatShortFileSize(context, oneHundredFiftyMB)
190→            }
191→
192→            fun createInstance(context: Context): CollectionIntegrityStorageCheck {
193→                val maybeCurrentCollectionSizeInBytes = getCollectionSize(context)
194→                if (maybeCurrentCollectionSizeInBytes == null) {
195→                    Timber.w("Error obtaining collection file size.")
196→                    val requiredFreeSpace = defaultRequiredFreeSpace(context)
197→                    return fromError(
198→                        context.resources.getString(
199→                            R.string.integrity_check_insufficient_space,
200→                            requiredFreeSpace
201→                        )
202→                    )
203→                }
204→
205→                // This means that when VACUUMing a database, as much as twice the size of the original database file is
206→                // required in free disk space. - https://www.sqlite.org/lang_vacuum.html
207→                val requiredSpaceInBytes = maybeCurrentCollectionSizeInBytes * 2
208→
209→                // We currently use the same directory as the collection for VACUUM/ANALYZE due to the SQLite APIs
210→                val collectionFile = File(getCollectionPath(context))
211→                val freeSpace = FileUtil.getFreeDiskSpace(collectionFile, -1)
212→                if (freeSpace == -1L) {
213→                    Timber.w("Error obtaining free space for '%s'", collectionFile.path)
214→                    val readableFileSize = Formatter.formatFileSize(context, requiredSpaceInBytes)
215→                    return fromError(
216→                        context.resources.getString(
217→                            R.string.integrity_check_insufficient_space,
218→                            readableFileSize
219→                        )
220→                    )
221→                }
222→                return CollectionIntegrityStorageCheck(requiredSpaceInBytes, freeSpace)
223→            }
224→        }
225→    }
226→
227→    enum class CollectionOpenFailure {
228→        FILE_TOO_NEW, CORRUPT, LOCKED, DISK_FULL
229→    }
230→
231→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
232→    @KotlinCleanup("maybe: call CollectionManager directly then remove method")
233→    fun setColForTests(col: Collection?) {
234→        CollectionManager.setColForTests(col)
235→    }
236→
237→    companion object {
238→        var instance: CollectionHelper = CollectionHelper()
239→            private set
240→
241→        @VisibleForTesting
242→        internal fun setInstanceForTesting(newInstance: CollectionHelper) {
243→            instance = newInstance
244→        }
245→
246→        // Name of anki2 file
247→        const val COLLECTION_FILENAME = "collection.anki2"
248→
249→        /**
250→         * The preference key for the path to the current AnkiDroid directory
251→         *
252→         * This directory contains all AnkiDroid data and media for a given collection
253→         * Except the Android preferences, cached files and [MetaDB]
254→         *
255→         * This can be changed by the [Preferences] screen
256→         * to allow a user to access a second collection via the same AnkiDroid app instance.
257→         *
258→         * The path also defines the collection that the AnkiDroid API accesses
259→         */
260→        const val PREF_COLLECTION_PATH = "deckPath"
261→        /**
262→         * If the last call to getColSafe() failed, this contains the error type.
263→         */
264→        /**
265→         * If the last call to getColSafe() failed, this stores the error type. This only exists
266→         * to enable better error reporting during startup; in the future it would be better if
267→         * callers check the exception themselves via a helper routine, instead of relying on a null
268→         * return.
269→         */
270→        var lastOpenFailure: CollectionOpenFailure? = null
271→            private set
272→
273→        fun getCollectionSize(context: Context): Long? {
274→            return try {
275→                val path = getCollectionPath(context)
276→                File(path).length()
277→            } catch (e: Exception) {
278→                Timber.e(e, "Error getting collection Length")
279→                null
280→            }
281→        }
282→
283→        /**
284→         * Create the AnkiDroid directory if it doesn't exist and add a .nomedia file to it if needed.
285→         *
286→         * The AnkiDroid directory is a user preference stored under [PREF_COLLECTION_PATH], and a sensible
287→         * default is chosen if the preference hasn't been created yet (i.e., on the first run).
288→         *
289→         * The presence of a .nomedia file indicates to media scanners that the directory must be
290→         * excluded from their search. We need to include this to avoid media scanners including
291→         * media files from the collection.media directory. The .nomedia file works at the directory
292→         * level, so placing it in the AnkiDroid directory will ensure media scanners will also exclude
293→         * the collection.media sub-directory.
294→         *
295→         * @param path  Directory to initialize
296→         * @throws StorageAccessException If no write access to directory
297→         */
298→        @Synchronized
299→        @Throws(StorageAccessException::class)
300→        fun initializeAnkiDroidDirectory(path: String) {
301→            // Create specified directory if it doesn't exit
302→            val dir = File(path)
303→            if (!dir.exists() && !dir.mkdirs()) {
304→                throw StorageAccessException("Failed to create AnkiDroid directory $path")
305→            }
306→            if (!dir.canWrite()) {
307→                throw StorageAccessException("No write access to AnkiDroid directory $path")
308→            }
309→            // Add a .nomedia file to it if it doesn't exist
310→            val nomedia = File(dir, ".nomedia")
311→            if (!nomedia.exists()) {
312→                try {
313→                    nomedia.createNewFile()
314→                } catch (e: IOException) {
315→                    throw StorageAccessException("Failed to create .nomedia file", e)
316→                }
317→            }
318→        }
319→
320→        /**
321→         * Try to access the current AnkiDroid directory
322→         * @return whether or not dir is accessible
323→         * @param context to get directory with
324→         */
325→        fun isCurrentAnkiDroidDirAccessible(context: Context): Boolean {
326→            return try {
327→                initializeAnkiDroidDirectory(getCurrentAnkiDroidDirectory(context))
328→                true
329→            } catch (e: StorageAccessException) {
330→                Timber.w(e)
331→                false
332→            }
333→        }
334→
335→        /**
336→         * Get the absolute path to a directory that is suitable to be the default starting location
337→         * for the AnkiDroid directory.
338→         *
339→         * Currently, this is a directory named "AnkiDroid" at the top level of the non-app-specific external storage directory.
340→         *
341→         * When targeting API > 29, AnkiDroid will have to use Scoped Storage on any device of any API level.
342→         * Scoped Storage only allows access to App-Specific directories (without permissions).
343→         * Hence, AnkiDroid won't be able to access the directory used currently on all devices,
344→         * regardless of their API level, once AnkiDroid targets API > 29.
345→         * Instead, AnkiDroid will have to use an App-Specific directory to store the AnkiDroid directory.
346→         * This applies to the entire AnkiDroid userbase.
347→         *
348→         * Currently, if `TESTING_SCOPED_STORAGE` is set to `true`, AnkiDroid uses its External
349→         * App-Specific directory.
350→         *
351→         *
352→         * External App-Specific Storage is used since the only advantage Internal App-Specific Storage has over External
353→         * App-Specific storage is additional security, but AnkiDroid does not store sensitive data. Defaulting to
354→         * External Storage preserves the current behavior of the App
355→         * (AnkiDroid defaults to External before the Migration To Scoped Storage).
356→         *
357→         *
358→         * TODO: If External Storage isn't emulated, allow users to choose between External & Internal App-Specific Storage
359→         * instead of defaulting to External App-Specific Storage. This should be done since using either one may be more
360→         * useful for them. If External Storage is emulated, there is no use in providing the option since Internal
361→         * Storage can not provide more storage space than External Storage if External Storage is emulated.
362→         *
363→         * See the detailed explanation on storage locations & their classification below for more details.
364→         *
365→         * App-Specific storage refers to directories which are meant to store files that are meant to be used by a
366→         * particular app. Each app has its own Internal & External App-Specific directory. Under Scoped Storage,
367→         * an app can only access its own Internal & External App-Specific directory without needing permissions.
368→         *
369→         * Storage can be classified as Internal or External Storage.
370→         *
371→         * Internal Storage: This storage is characterized by the fact that it is always available since it always resides
372→         * on the device's own non-removable storage.
373→         *
374→         *
375→         * App-Specific Internal Storage can be accessed by ONLY the app which owns that directory (without any permissions).
376→         * It cannot be accessed by any other apps.
377→         * It cannot be accessed using the Files app on Android or by connecting a device to a pc via USB.
378→         *
379→         * External Storage:
380→         *
381→         * This storage is characterized only by the fact that it is not guaranteed to be available.
382→         *
383→         * It may be built-in, non-removable storage on the device which is being emulated to function like external storage.
384→         * In this case, it doesn't offer more space than Internal Storage.
385→         *
386→         * Or, it may be removable storage like an SD Card.
387→         *
388→         * App-Specific External Storage can be accessed by the app it is owned by without any permissions.
389→         * It can be accessed by any apps with the WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permission.
390→         * It can also be accessed via the Android Files app or by connecting the device to a PC via USB.
391→         *
392→         * Note: The Files app can be misleading. On Samsung devices, clicking on Internal Storage it actually shows the
393→         * emulated external storage (/storage/emulated/0/ in my case) - this is because from the point of view of the user,
394→         * emulated external storage is just more internal storage since it is built into the phone. This is why vendors
395→         * like Samsung may refer to external emulated storage as internal storage, even though for developers, they mean
396→         * very different things as explained above.
397→         *
398→         * @return Absolute Path to the default location starting location for the AnkiDroid directory
399→         */
400→        // TODO Tracked in https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/5304
401→        @CheckResult
402→        fun getDefaultAnkiDroidDirectory(context: Context): String {
403→            val legacyStorage = selectAnkiDroidFolder(context) != AppPrivateFolder
404→            return if (!legacyStorage) {
405→                File(getAppSpecificExternalAnkiDroidDirectory(context), "AnkiDroid").absolutePath
406→            } else {
407→                legacyAnkiDroidDirectory
408→            }
409→        }
410→
411→        /**
412→         * Returns the absolute path to the AnkiDroid directory under the primary/shared external storage directory.
413→         * This directory may be in emulated external storage, or can be an SD Card directory.
414→         *
415→         *
416→         * The path returned will no longer be accessible to AnkiDroid once targetSdk > 29
417→         *
418→         * @return Absolute path to the AnkiDroid directory in primary shared/external storage
419→         */
420→        val legacyAnkiDroidDirectory: String
421→            get() = File(Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory(), "AnkiDroid").absolutePath
422→
423→        /**
424→         * Returns the absolute path to the AnkiDroid directory under the app-specific, primary/shared external storage
425→         * directory.
426→         *
427→         *
428→         * This directory may be in emulated external storage, or can be an SD Card directory.
429→         * If it is actually external storage, i.e., removable storage like an SD Card, instead of storage
430→         * built into the device itself, using this directory over internal storage can be beneficial since
431→         * it may be able to store more data.
432→         *
433→         *
434→         * AnkiDroid can access this directory without permissions, even under Scoped Storage
435→         * Other apps can access this directory if they have the WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permission
436→         *
437→         * @param context Used to get the External App-Specific directory for AnkiDroid
438→         * @return Returns the absolute path to the App-Specific External AnkiDroid directory
439→         */
440→        private fun getAppSpecificExternalAnkiDroidDirectory(context: Context): String {
441→            return context.getExternalFilesDir(null)!!.absolutePath
442→        }
443→
444→        /**
445→         * @return Returns an array of [File]s reflecting the directories that AnkiDroid can access without storage permissions
446→         * @see android.content.Context.getExternalFilesDirs
447→         */
448→        fun getAppSpecificExternalDirectories(context: Context): List<File> {
449→            return context.getExternalFilesDirs(null).filterNotNull()
450→        }
451→
452→        /**
453→         * Returns the absolute path to the private AnkiDroid directory under the app-specific, internal storage directory.
454→         *
455→         *
456→         * AnkiDroid can access this directory without permissions, even under Scoped Storage
457→         * Other apps cannot access this directory, regardless of what permissions they have
458→         *
459→         * @param context Used to get the Internal App-Specific directory for AnkiDroid
460→         * @return Returns the absolute path to the App-Specific Internal AnkiDroid Directory
461→         */
462→        fun getAppSpecificInternalAnkiDroidDirectory(context: Context): String {
463→            return context.filesDir.absolutePath
464→        }
465→
466→        /**
467→         *
468→         * @return the path to the actual [Collection] file
469→         */
470→        fun getCollectionPath(context: Context): String {
471→            return File(getCurrentAnkiDroidDirectory(context), COLLECTION_FILENAME).absolutePath
472→        }
473→
474→        /** A temporary override for [getCurrentAnkiDroidDirectory] */
475→        var ankiDroidDirectoryOverride: String? = null
476→
477→        /**
478→         * @return the absolute path to the AnkiDroid directory.
479→         */
480→        fun getCurrentAnkiDroidDirectory(context: Context): String {
481→            return getCurrentAnkiDroidDirectoryOptionalContext(context.sharedPrefs()) { context }
482→        }
483→
484→        fun getMediaDirectory(context: Context): File {
485→            return File(getCurrentAnkiDroidDirectory(context), "collection.media")
486→        }
487→
488→        /**
489→         * An accessor which makes [Context] optional in the case that [PREF_COLLECTION_PATH] is set
490→         *
491→         * @return the absolute path to the AnkiDroid directory.
492→         */
493→        // This uses a lambda as we typically depends on the `lateinit` AnkiDroidApp.instance
494→        // If we remove all Android references, we get a significant unit test speedup
495→        @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
496→        internal fun getCurrentAnkiDroidDirectoryOptionalContext(preferences: SharedPreferences, context: () -> Context): String {
497→            return if (AnkiDroidApp.INSTRUMENTATION_TESTING) {
498→                // create an "androidTest" directory inside the current collection directory which contains the test data
499→                // "/AnkiDroid/androidTest" would be a new collection path
500→                val currentCollectionDirectory = preferences.getOrSetString(PREF_COLLECTION_PATH) {
501→                    getDefaultAnkiDroidDirectory(context())
502→                }
503→                File(
504→                    currentCollectionDirectory,
505→                    "androidTest"
506→                ).absolutePath
507→            } else if (ankiDroidDirectoryOverride != null) {
508→                ankiDroidDirectoryOverride!!
509→            } else {
510→                preferences.getOrSetString(PREF_COLLECTION_PATH) {
511→                    getDefaultAnkiDroidDirectory(
512→                        context()
513→                    )
514→                }
515→            }
516→        }
517→
518→        /**
519→         * Resets the AnkiDroid directory to the [getDefaultAnkiDroidDirectory]
520→         * Note: if [android.R.attr.preserveLegacyExternalStorage] is in use
521→         * this will represent a change from `/AnkiDroid` to `/Android/data/...`
522→         */
523→        fun resetAnkiDroidDirectory(context: Context) {
524→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
525→            val directory = getDefaultAnkiDroidDirectory(context)
526→            Timber.d("resetting AnkiDroid directory to %s", directory)
527→            preferences.edit { putString(PREF_COLLECTION_PATH, directory) }
528→        }
529→
530→        @Throws(UnknownDatabaseVersionException::class)
531→        fun getDatabaseVersion(context: Context): Int {
532→            // backend can't open a schema version outside range, so fall back to a pure DB implementation
533→            val colPath = getCollectionPath(context)
534→            if (!File(colPath).exists()) {
535→                throw UnknownDatabaseVersionException(FileNotFoundException(colPath))
536→            }
537→            var db: DB? = null
538→            return try {
539→                db = DB.withAndroidFramework(context, colPath)
540→                db.queryScalar("SELECT ver FROM col")
541→            } catch (e: Exception) {
542→                Timber.w(e, "Couldn't open the database to obtain collection version!")
543→                throw UnknownDatabaseVersionException(e)
544→            } finally {
545→                db?.close()
546→            }
547→        }
548→    }
549→}

 18→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19→
 20→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→
 26→const val PORT = 0
 27→// const val PORT = 40001
 28→
 29→// local debugging:
 30→// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31→
 32→open class AnkiServer(
 33→    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34→) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35→
 36→    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37→        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41→    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42→    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43→
 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        return when (session.method) {
 46→            Method.POST -> {
 47→                val uri = session.uri
 48→                Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 49→                val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 50→                buildResponse {
 51→                    postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 52→                }
 53→            }
 54→            Method.GET -> newFixedLengthResponse(Response.Status.NOT_FOUND, null, null)
 55→            else -> newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 56→        }
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun buildResponse(
 60→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 61→    ): Response {
 62→        return try {
 63→            val data = runBlocking {
 64→                block()
 65→            }
 66→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 67→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 68→            Timber.w(exc, "buildResponse failure")
 69→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 70→        }
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    companion object {
 74→        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 75→
 76→        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 77→        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 78→        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 79→
 80→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 81→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 82→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 83→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 84→            return bytes
 85→        }
 86→
 87→        fun newChunkedResponse(
 88→            data: ByteArray?,
 89→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
 90→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
 91→        ): Response {
 92→            return if (data == null) {
 93→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 94→            } else {
 95→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
 96→            }
 97→        }
 98→    }
 99→}

24→import java.io.File
25→import java.io.FileInputStream
26→
27→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
28→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
29→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
30→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
31→        .setDomain(domain)
32→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
33→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
34→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
35→            }
36→            try {
37→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()
46→}

conversationHistory.content:
Now I understand the root cause. The issue is:

1. The HTML is loaded with `baseUrl` set to `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/` (from AnkiServer)
2. SVG files are loaded as `<object data="_keyboard.svg">` tags
3. Due to CORS restrictions, JavaScript cannot access `img_obj.contentDocument` when the SVG is loaded via HTTP URL
4. AnkiServer currently only handles POST requests, not GET requests for media files

The solution based on the issue discussion is to:
1. Enable GET request handling in AnkiServer to serve media files
2. Add appropriate CORS headers to allow JavaScript access to SVG content

Let me examine the current AnkiServer implementation and add GET request support:

 44→            .title = CollectionManager.TR.preferencesBackups()
 45→
 46→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_advanced_screen_key).apply {
 47→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
 48→                isVisible = false
 49→            }
 50→        }
 51→
 52→        if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(requireContext())) {
 53→            setDevOptionsVisibility(true)
 54→        }
 55→
 56→        configureSearchBar(
 57→            requireActivity() as AppCompatActivity,
 58→            requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key).searchConfiguration
 59→        )
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun highlightHeaderPreference(headerPreference: HeaderPreference) {
 63→        if (!(activity as Preferences).hasLateralNavigation()) {
 64→            return
 65→        }
 66→        selectedHeaderPreference?.setHighlighted(false)
 67→        // highlight the newly selected header
 68→        selectedHeaderPreference = headerPreference.apply {
 69→            setHighlighted(true)
 70→            selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = this.key
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 75→        highlightHeaderPreference(preference as HeaderPreference)
 76→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
 80→        super.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
 81→        outState.putString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF, selectedHeaderPreferenceKey)
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    override fun onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 85→        super.onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState)
 86→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun onStart() {
 90→        super.onStart()
 91→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.settings)
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 95→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 96→        // use the same fragment container to search in case there is a navigation container
 97→        requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key)
 98→            .searchConfiguration
 99→            .setFragmentContainerViewId((view.parent as? ViewGroup)?.id ?: R.id.settings_container)
100→    }
101→
102→    fun setDevOptionsVisibility(isVisible: Boolean) {
103→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_dev_options_screen_key).isVisible = isVisible
104→    }
105→
106→    companion object {
107→        private const val KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF = "selected_header_pref"
108→        private const val DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER = "generalScreen"
109→
110→        fun configureSearchBar(activity: AppCompatActivity, searchConfiguration: SearchConfiguration) {
111→            with(searchConfiguration) {
112→                setActivity(activity)
113→                setBreadcrumbsEnabled(true)
114→                setFuzzySearchEnabled(false)
115→                setHistoryEnabled(true)
116→
117→                index(R.xml.preferences_general)
118→                index(R.xml.preferences_reviewing)
119→                index(R.xml.preferences_sync)
120→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_sync_server)
121→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_sync)
122→                index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
123→                index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)
124→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_buttons)
125→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_appearance)
126→                index(R.xml.preferences_controls)
127→                index(R.xml.preferences_accessibility)
128→            }
129→
130→            // Some preferences and categories are only shown conditionally,
131→            // so they should be searchable based on the same conditions
132→
133→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
134→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(activity)) {
135→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_dev_options)
136→                /** From [DevOptionsFragment.initSubscreen] */
137→                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
138→                    searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.dev_options_enabled_by_user_key))
139→                }
140→            }
141→
142→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
143→            if (!AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
144→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_advanced)
145→            }
146→
147→            /** From [NotificationsSettingsFragment.initSubscreen] */
148→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
149→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_vibrate_key))
150→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_blink_key))
151→            }
152→
153→            /** From [AdvancedSettingsFragment.removeUnnecessaryAdvancedPrefs] */
154→            if (!CompatHelper.hasScrollKeys()) {
155→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.double_scrolling_gap_key))
156→            }
157→
158→            searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.user_actions_controls_category_key))
159→        }
160→    }
161→}
162→

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.os.Bundle
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
 22→import androidx.preference.Preference
 23→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 24→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchConfiguration
 25→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchPreference
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 29→import com.ichi2.compat.CompatHelper
 30→import com.ichi2.preferences.HeaderPreference
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.AdaptionUtil
 32→
 33→class HeaderFragment : PreferenceFragmentCompat() {
 34→    private var selectedHeaderPreference: HeaderPreference? = null
 35→    private var selectedHeaderPreferenceKey: String = DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 36→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 37→        setPreferencesFromResource(R.xml.preference_headers, rootKey)
 38→
 39→        selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = savedInstanceState?.getString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF) ?: DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 40→
 41→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 42→
 43→        requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(R.string.pref_backup_limits_screen_key)
 44→            .title = CollectionManager.TR.preferencesBackups()
 45→
 46→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_advanced_screen_key).apply {
 47→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
 48→                isVisible = false
 49→            }
 50→        }
 51→
 52→        if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(requireContext())) {
 53→            setDevOptionsVisibility(true)
 54→        }
 55→
 56→        configureSearchBar(
 57→            requireActivity() as AppCompatActivity,
 58→            requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key).searchConfiguration
 59→        )
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun highlightHeaderPreference(headerPreference: HeaderPreference) {
 63→        if (!(activity as Preferences).hasLateralNavigation()) {
 64→            return
 65→        }
 66→        selectedHeaderPreference?.setHighlighted(false)
 67→        // highlight the newly selected header
 68→        selectedHeaderPreference = headerPreference.apply {
 69→            setHighlighted(true)
 70→            selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = this.key
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 75→        highlightHeaderPreference(preference as HeaderPreference)
 76→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
 80→        super.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
 81→        outState.putString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF, selectedHeaderPreferenceKey)
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    override fun onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 85→        super.onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState)
 86→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun onStart() {
 90→        super.onStart()
 91→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.settings)
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 95→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 96→        // use the same fragment container to search in case there is a navigation container
 97→        requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key)
 98→            .searchConfiguration
 99→            .setFragmentContainerViewId((view.parent as? ViewGroup)?.id ?: R.id.settings_container)
100→    }
101→
102→    fun setDevOptionsVisibility(isVisible: Boolean) {
103→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_dev_options_screen_key).isVisible = isVisible
104→    }
105→
106→    companion object {
107→        private const val KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF = "selected_header_pref"
108→        private const val DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER = "generalScreen"
109→
110→        fun configureSearchBar(activity: AppCompatActivity, searchConfiguration: SearchConfiguration) {
111→            with(searchConfiguration) {
112→                setActivity(activity)
113→                setBreadcrumbsEnabled(true)
114→                setFuzzySearchEnabled(false)
115→                setHistoryEnabled(true)
116→
117→                index(R.xml.preferences_general)
118→                index(R.xml.preferences_reviewing)
119→                index(R.xml.preferences_sync)
120→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_sync_server)
121→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_sync)
122→                index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
123→                index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)
124→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_buttons)
125→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_appearance)
126→                index(R.xml.preferences_controls)
127→                index(R.xml.preferences_accessibility)
128→            }
129→
130→            // Some preferences and categories are only shown conditionally,
131→            // so they should be searchable based on the same conditions
132→
133→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
134→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(activity)) {
135→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_dev_options)
136→                /** From [DevOptionsFragment.initSubscreen] */
137→                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
138→                    searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.dev_options_enabled_by_user_key))
139→                }
140→            }
141→
142→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
143→            if (!AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
144→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_advanced)
145→            }
146→
147→            /** From [NotificationsSettingsFragment.initSubscreen] */
148→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
149→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_vibrate_key))
150→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_blink_key))
151→            }
152→
153→            /** From [AdvancedSettingsFragment.removeUnnecessaryAdvancedPrefs] */
154→            if (!CompatHelper.hasScrollKeys()) {
155→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.double_scrolling_gap_key))
156→            }
157→
158→            searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.user_actions_controls_category_key))
159→        }
160→    }
161→}
162→

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.171 (commit: 6b1ba50b).

## Issue to Resolve

Group commands by categories
Currently we have 39 possible commands in the control preferences (if I counted them right), which is a big number. 

Besides searching, to find a command more easily, I suggest grouping them in some categories, like:

- Answer buttons
    - Show answer
    - Answer button 1
    - Answer button 2
    - ...
- Flags
    - red
    - blue
    - orange
    - ...
- Whiteboard
    - ...
- Current card/note
    - suspend
    - delete
    - bury
    - ...
- Navigation
    - Page up
    - Page down
    - Abort
    - ...
- Misc
    - whatever couldn't be put in any other categories

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Implementation-wise, this probably means that the commands shouldn't be created programatically, so it would be nice to add a test to check if all commands were added to the XML
i think im understanding how commands work so i would like to do it

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/ControlsSettingsFragment.kt`

- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_controls.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 34→
 35→/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
 36→enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 37→    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 38→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.gesture_answer_1),
 39→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.gesture_answer_2),
 40→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.gesture_answer_3),
 41→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.gesture_answer_4),

 44→    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note),
 45→    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
 46→    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
 47→    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
 48→    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
 49→    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
 50→    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 51→    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 52→    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
 53→    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
 54→    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
 55→    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
 56→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
 57→    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
 58→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
 59→    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
 60→    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
 61→    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
 62→    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
 63→    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
 64→    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
 65→    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
 66→    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
 67→    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
 68→    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
 69→    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
 70→    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
 71→    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
 72→    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
 73→    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);
 74→
 75→    companion object {
 76→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 77→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())
 78→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 79→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    val preferenceKey: String
 83→        get() = "binding_$name"
 84→
 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
123→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
124→        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
125→            when (this) {
126→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(
127→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
128→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
129→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
130→                )
131→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(
132→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
133→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
134→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
135→                )
136→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(
137→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
141→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
142→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
144→                )
145→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
146→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
147→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
149→                )
150→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
151→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
152→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
153→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
154→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
155→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
156→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
157→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
158→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
159→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
161→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
162→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
163→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
164→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
165→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
166→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
167→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
168→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
169→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
170→                else -> ArrayList()
171→            }
172→
173→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
174→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
178→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
182→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
186→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
187→    }
188→
189→    fun interface CommandProcessor {
190→        /**
191→         *
192→         * example failure: answering an ease on the front of the card
193→         */
194→        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
195→    }
196→}

16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
17→
18→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
19→import com.ichi2.anki.R
20→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
21→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
22→import com.ichi2.preferences.ControlPreference
23→
24→class ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
25→    override val preferenceResource: Int
26→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
27→    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
28→        get() = "prefs.controls"
29→
30→    override fun initSubscreen() {
31→        val commandMappingCategory = requirePreference<PreferenceCategory>(R.string.controls_command_mapping_cat_key)
32→        addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(commandMappingCategory)
33→    }
34→
35→    /** Attaches all possible [ControlPreference] elements to a given [PreferenceCategory] */
36→    fun addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(category: PreferenceCategory) {
37→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
38→            val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
39→                setTitle(command.resourceId)
40→                key = command.preferenceKey
41→                setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
42→            }
43→            category.addPreference(preference)
44→        }
45→    }
46→}
