 10→ *  WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A
 11→ *  PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
 12→ *
 13→ *  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15→ */
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 20→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 21→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.TR
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.Reviewer
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.AnswerButtons.AGAIN
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.AnswerButtons.GOOD
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.AnswerButtons.HARD
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.utils.ext.secondsToShowAnswer
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.utils.ext.secondsToShowQuestion
 31→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 32→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 33→import com.ichi2.libanki.DeckConfig
 34→import com.ichi2.libanki.DeckId
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.HandlerUtils
 36→import timber.log.Timber
 37→
 38→/**
 39→ * AnkiDroid contains a setting: "Automatic display answer" which displays the
 40→ * question/answer automatically after a set period of time.
 41→ *
 42→ * ## Settings
 43→ * See: [AutomaticAnswerSettings]
 44→ *
 45→ * ## Implementation
 46→ *
 47→ * If "Enabled" is disabled, then nothing happens
 48→ * If Time to show Answer is 0, the automatic flipping code is disabled
 49→ * If Time to show next question is 0, the automatic answering code is disabled
 50→ *
 51→ * When a card is displayed (Front)
 52→ *
 53→ * If it has neither TTS nor audio, schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 54→ * If it has audio, calculate the time for the audio to play
 55→ *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" + "audio runtime" seconds
 56→ * If it has TTS, add a handler to the completion of TTS
 57→ *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 58→ *
 59→ * Do the same for the Back, but use the "Time to show next question" setting.
 60→ *
 61→ * If the "next question" is shown, "Again" is pressed.
 62→ *
 63→ * The implementation originally had multiple bugs regarding double presses of the answer buttons.
 64→ * This is because the user can press show the card at the same instant that the automatic code executes
 65→ *
 66→ * This has been worked around via the calls: [onSelectEase] and [onShowAnswer].
 67→ * Both of which clear the task queue for the question ([onSelectEase] or vice-versa)
 68→ */
 69→class AutomaticAnswer(
 70→    target: AutomaticallyAnswered,
 71→    @VisibleForTesting val settings: AutomaticAnswerSettings
 72→) {
 73→
 74→    /** Whether any tasks should be executed/scheduled.
 75→     *
 76→     * Ensures that auto answer does not occur if the reviewer is minimised
 77→     */
 78→    var isDisabled: Boolean = false
 79→        private set
 80→
 81→    /**
 82→     * Whether the sounds for the current side of the card have been played
 83→     * So [scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer] and [scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion] do nothing
 84→     * if called multiple times on the same side of the card
 85→     */
 86→    private var hasPlayedSounds: Boolean = false
 87→
 88→    private val showAnswerTask = Runnable {
 89→        if (isDisabled) {
 90→            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
 91→            return@Runnable
 92→        }
 93→        target.automaticShowAnswer()
 94→    }
 95→    private val showQuestionTask = Runnable {
 96→        if (isDisabled) {
 97→            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
 98→            return@Runnable
 99→        }
100→        target.automaticShowQuestion(settings.answerAction)
101→    }
102→
103→    /**
104→     * Handler for the delay in auto showing question and/or answer
105→     * One toggle for both question and answer, could set longer delay for auto next question
106→     */
107→    @VisibleForTesting
108→    val timeoutHandler = HandlerUtils.newHandler()
109→
110→    @VisibleForTesting
111→    fun delayedShowQuestion(delay: Long) {
112→        if (isDisabled) {
113→            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
114→            return
115→        }
116→        Timber.i("Automatically showing question in %dms", delay)
117→        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showQuestionTask, delay)
118→    }
119→
120→    @VisibleForTesting
121→    fun delayedShowAnswer(delay: Long) {
122→        if (isDisabled) {
123→            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
124→            return
125→        }
126→        Timber.i("Automatically showing answer in %dms", delay)
127→        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showAnswerTask, delay)
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun stopShowQuestionTask() {
131→        Timber.i("stop: automatically show question")
132→        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showQuestionTask)
133→    }
134→
135→    private fun stopShowAnswerTask() {
136→        Timber.i("stop: automatically show answer")
137→        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showAnswerTask)
138→    }
139→
140→    fun enable() {
141→        isDisabled = false
142→    }
143→
144→    fun disable() {
145→        isDisabled = true
146→        stopShowAnswerTask()
147→        stopShowQuestionTask()
148→    }
149→
150→    /** Stop any "Automatic show answer" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
151→    fun onDisplayQuestion() {
152→        if (!settings.useTimer) return
153→        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion) return
154→        hasPlayedSounds = false
155→
156→        stopShowAnswerTask()
157→    }
158→
159→    /** Stop any "Automatic show question" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
160→    fun onDisplayAnswer() {
161→        if (!settings.useTimer) return
162→        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer) return
163→        hasPlayedSounds = false
164→
165→        stopShowQuestionTask()
166→    }
167→
168→    // region TODO: These attempt to stop a race condition between a manual answer and the automated answer
169→    // I don't believe this is thread-safe
170→
171→    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has selected an answer */
172→    fun onSelectEase() {
173→        Timber.v("onSelectEase")
174→        stopShowQuestionTask()
175→    }
176→
177→    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has flipped the card */
178→    fun onShowAnswer() {
179→        Timber.v("onShowAnswer")
180→        stopShowAnswerTask()
181→    }
182→
183→    // endregion
184→
185→    /**
186→     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowAnswer]
187→     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
188→     */
189→    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer(additionalDelay: Long = 0) {
190→        if (!settings.useTimer) return
191→        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion) return
192→        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
193→        hasPlayedSounds = true
194→        delayedShowAnswer(settings.millisecondsToShowQuestionFor + additionalDelay)
195→    }
196→
197→    /**
198→     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowQuestion]
199→     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
200→     */
201→    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion(additionalMediaDelay: Long = 0) {
202→        if (!settings.useTimer) return
203→        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer) return
204→        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
205→        hasPlayedSounds = true
206→        delayedShowQuestion(settings.millisecondsToShowAnswerFor + additionalMediaDelay)
207→    }
208→
209→    fun isEnabled(): Boolean {
210→        return settings.useTimer
211→    }
212→
213→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
214→    internal fun simulateCardFlip() {
215→        Timber.d("simulateCardFlip")
216→        hasPlayedSounds = false
217→    }
218→
219→    interface AutomaticallyAnswered {
220→        fun automaticShowAnswer()
221→        fun automaticShowQuestion(action: AutomaticAnswerAction)
222→    }
223→
224→    companion object {
225→        @CheckResult
226→        fun defaultInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered): AutomaticAnswer {
227→            return AutomaticAnswer(target, AutomaticAnswerSettings())
228→        }
229→
230→        @CheckResult
231→        fun createInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered, preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswer {
232→            val settings = AutomaticAnswerSettings.createInstance(preferences, col)
233→            return AutomaticAnswer(target, settings)
234→        }
235→    }
236→}
237→
238→/**
239→ * ### Reviewing - Automatic display answer
240→ *
241→ * * Enabled
242→ * * Time to show answer (20s)
243→ * * Time to show next question (60s)
244→ * * Automatic action: the action to take when reviewing
245→ *
246→ * ### Deck Options - Reviews - Automatic Display Answer
247→ *
248→ * These settings also exist in an options group
249→ * Although they're under "reviews", it counts for all cards
250→ *
251→ * * Use General settings (using the settings above - enabled by default)
252→ * * Enabled, etc...
253→ */
254→class AutomaticAnswerSettings(
255→    val answerAction: AutomaticAnswerAction = AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD,
256→    @get:JvmName("useTimer") val useTimer: Boolean = false,
257→    private val secondsToShowQuestionFor: Double = 60.0,
258→    private val secondsToShowAnswerFor: Double = 20.0
259→) {
260→
261→    val millisecondsToShowQuestionFor = (secondsToShowQuestionFor * 1000L).toLong()
262→    val millisecondsToShowAnswerFor = (secondsToShowAnswerFor * 1000L).toLong()
263→
264→    // a wait of zero means auto-advance is disabled
265→    val autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer; get() = secondsToShowAnswerFor > 0
266→    val autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion; get() = secondsToShowQuestionFor > 0
267→
268→    companion object {
269→        /**
270→         * Obtains the options for [AutomaticAnswer] in the deck config
271→         */
272→        @NeedsTest("ensure question setting maps to question parameter")
273→        fun queryOptions(
274→            preferences: SharedPreferences,
275→            col: Collection,
276→            selectedDid: DeckId
277→        ): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
278→            val conf = col.decks.configDictForDeckId(selectedDid)
279→            val action = getAction(conf)
280→            val useTimer = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
281→
282→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(
283→                answerAction = action,
284→                useTimer = useTimer,
285→                secondsToShowQuestionFor = conf.secondsToShowQuestion,
286→                secondsToShowAnswerFor = conf.secondsToShowAnswer
287→            )
288→        }
289→
290→        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
291→            return queryOptions(preferences, col, col.decks.selected())
292→        }
293→
294→        private fun getAction(conf: DeckConfig): AutomaticAnswerAction {
295→            return try {
296→                val value: Int = conf.optInt(AutomaticAnswerAction.CONFIG_KEY)
297→                AutomaticAnswerAction.fromConfigValue(value)
298→            } catch (e: Exception) {
299→                AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
300→            }
301→        }
302→    }
303→}
304→
305→/**
306→ * Represents a value from [anki.deck_config.DeckConfig.Config.AnswerAction]
307→ * Executed when answering a card (showing the question).
308→ */
309→enum class AutomaticAnswerAction(private val configValue: Int) {
310→    /** Default: least invasive action */
311→    BURY_CARD(0),
312→    ANSWER_AGAIN(1),
313→    ANSWER_GOOD(2),
314→    ANSWER_HARD(3),
315→    SHOW_REMINDER(4);
316→
317→    fun execute(reviewer: Reviewer) {
318→        val action = this.toCommand()
319→        if (action != null) {
320→            Timber.i("Executing %s", action)
321→            reviewer.executeCommand(action)
322→        } else {
323→            reviewer.showSnackbar(TR.studyingAnswerTimeElapsed())
324→        }
325→    }
326→
327→    /** Convert to a [ViewerCommand] */
328→    private fun toCommand(): ViewerCommand? {
329→        return when (this) {
330→            BURY_CARD -> ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD
331→            ANSWER_AGAIN -> AGAIN.toViewerCommand()
332→            ANSWER_HARD -> HARD.toViewerCommand()
333→            ANSWER_GOOD -> GOOD.toViewerCommand()
334→            else -> null
335→        }
336→    }
337→
338→    companion object {
339→        /**
340→         * An integer representing the action when Automatic Answer flips a card from answer to question
341→         *
342→         * @see AutomaticAnswerAction
343→         */
344→        const val CONFIG_KEY = "answerAction"
345→
346→        /** convert from [anki.deck_config.DeckConfig.Config.AnswerAction] to the enum */
347→        fun fromConfigValue(i: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
348→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.configValue == i } ?: BURY_CARD
349→        }
350→    }
351→}
352→

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.218 (commit: b0d37589).

## Issue to Resolve

User report: Can't see or open CSVs
### Checked for duplicates?

### Does it also happen in the desktop version?

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

Exact steps unknown

https://www.reddit.com/r/Anki/comments/1i8b5p3/best_way_to_easily_create_flashcards_on_ankidroid/

User is on Discord and responsive: https://discord.com/channels/368267295601983490/701939078786187315/1332453507789357116

Requested a screenshot of the file in the location, and a screenshot of AnkiDroid not picking it up

### Expected behaviour

The CSV file is visible

While testing, I was having huge problems with 'saving' CSVs in Google Drive, but they didn't appear in the folder I saved them in.

I don't trust files on Android at all

### Research

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:

 11→ * PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.             *
 12→ *                                                                                      *

 14→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.                           *
 15→ ****************************************************************************************/
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.Intent
 21→import android.net.Uri
 22→import android.os.Bundle
 23→import android.os.Message
 24→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 25→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 26→import androidx.core.app.TaskStackBuilder
 27→import androidx.core.content.FileProvider
 28→import androidx.core.content.IntentCompat
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandler.Companion.storeMessage
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandlerMessage
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.ScopedStorageService
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.services.ReminderService
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.reviewer.ReviewerFragment
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.worker.SyncWorker
 36→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 37→import com.ichi2.libanki.DeckId
 38→import com.ichi2.utils.FileUtil
 39→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.handleFileImport
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.isInvalidViewIntent
 41→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.showImportUnsuccessfulDialog
 42→import com.ichi2.utils.IntentUtil.resolveMimeType
 43→import com.ichi2.utils.NetworkUtils
 44→import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions
 45→import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions.hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission
 46→import com.ichi2.utils.copyToClipboard
 47→import com.ichi2.utils.trimToLength
 48→import timber.log.Timber
 49→import java.io.File
 50→import kotlin.math.max
 51→import kotlin.math.min
 52→
 53→/**
 54→ * Class which handles how the application responds to different intents, forcing it to always be single task,
 55→ * but allowing custom behavior depending on the intent
 56→ * It inherits from [AbstractIntentHandler]
 57→ *
 58→ * @author Tim
 59→ */
 60→class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
 61→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 62→        // Note: This is our entry point from the launcher with intent: android.intent.action.MAIN
 63→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 64→        val intent = intent
 65→        Timber.v(intent.toString())
 66→        val reloadIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
 67→        reloadIntent.setDataAndType(getIntent().data, getIntent().type)
 68→        val action = intent.action
 69→        // #6157 - We want to block actions that need permissions we don't have, but not the default case
 70→        // as this requires nothing
 71→        val runIfStoragePermissions = { runnable: () -> Unit -> performActionIfStorageAccessible(reloadIntent, action) { runnable() } }
 72→        val launchType = getLaunchType(intent)
 73→        // TODO block the UI with some kind of ProgressDialog instead of cancelling the sync work
 74→        if (requiresCollectionAccess(launchType)) {
 75→            SyncWorker.cancel(this)
 76→        }
 77→        when (launchType) {
 78→            LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT ->
 79→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 80→                    handleFileImport(fileIntent, reloadIntent, action)
 81→                    finish()
 82→                }
 83→            LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT ->
 84→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 85→                    onSelectedCsvForImport(fileIntent)
 86→                    finish()
 87→                }
 88→            LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT ->
 89→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 90→                    handleImageImport(intent)
 91→                    finish()
 92→                }
 93→            LaunchType.SYNC -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleSyncIntent(reloadIntent, action) }
 94→            LaunchType.REVIEW -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleReviewIntent(reloadIntent, intent) }
 95→            LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW -> {
 96→                Timber.d("onCreate() performing default action")
 97→                launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
 98→            }
 99→            LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> {
100→                copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent)
101→                finish()
102→            }
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent: Intent) {
107→        Timber.i("Copying debug info to clipboard")
108→        // null string is handled by copyToClipboard in try-catch
109→        this.copyToClipboard(
110→            text = (intent.getStringExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA)!!),
111→            failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info,
112→        )
113→    }
114→
115→    private val fileIntent: Intent
116→        get() {
117→            return if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
118→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Intent::class.java) ?: intent
119→            } else {
120→                intent
121→            }
122→        }
123→
124→    /**
125→     * Execute the runnable if one of the two following conditions are satisfied:
126→     *
127→     *  * AnkiDroid is using an app-private directory to store user data
128→     *  * AnkiDroid is using a legacy directory to store user data but has access to it since storage permission
129→     * has been granted (as long as AnkiDroid targeted API < 30, requested legacy storage, and has not been uninstalled since)
130→     *
131→     */
132→    @NeedsTest("clicking a file in 'Files' to import")
133→    private fun performActionIfStorageAccessible(
134→        reloadIntent: Intent,
135→        action: String?,
136→        block: () -> Unit,
137→    ) {
138→        if (grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {
139→            Timber.i("User has storage permissions. Running intent: %s", action)
140→            block()
141→        } else {
142→            Timber.i("No Storage Permission, cancelling intent '%s'", action)
143→            launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun handleReviewIntent(
148→        reloadIntent: Intent,
149→        reviewerIntent: Intent,
150→    ) {
151→        val deckId = intent.getLongExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, 0)
152→        Timber.i("Handling intent to review deck '%d'", deckId)
153→
154→        val reviewIntent =
155→            if (sharedPrefs().getBoolean("newReviewer", false)) {
156→                ReviewerFragment.getIntent(this)
157→            } else {
158→                Intent(this, Reviewer::class.java).apply {
159→                    addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
160→                }
161→            }
162→        CollectionManager.getColUnsafe().decks.select(deckId)
163→        // Clean the stack out under the reviewer to avoid any incorrect activities / dialogs /
164→        // data state from prior app usage showing after reviewer exits if going to reviewer directly
165→        TaskStackBuilder
166→            .create(applicationContext)
167→            .addNextIntent(reloadIntent)
168→            .addNextIntent(reviewIntent)
169→            .startActivities()
170→        finish()
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun handleSyncIntent(
174→        reloadIntent: Intent,
175→        action: String?,
176→    ) {
177→        Timber.i("Handling Sync Intent")
178→        sendDoSyncMsg()
179→        reloadIntent.action = action
180→        reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
181→        startActivity(reloadIntent)
182→        finish()
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun handleFileImport(
186→        intent: Intent,
187→        reloadIntent: Intent,
188→        action: String?,
189→    ) {
190→        Timber.i("Handling file import")
191→        if (!hasShownAppIntro()) {
192→            Timber.i("Trying to import a file when the app was not started at all")
193→            showThemedToast(this, R.string.app_not_initialized_new, false)
194→            return
195→        }
196→        val importResult = handleFileImport(this, intent)
197→        // attempt to delete the downloaded deck if it is a shared deck download import
198→        if (intent.hasExtra(SharedDecksDownloadFragment.EXTRA_IS_SHARED_DOWNLOAD)) {
199→            deleteDownloadedDeck(intent.data)
200→        }
201→
202→        // Start DeckPicker if we correctly processed ACTION_VIEW
203→        if (importResult.isSuccess) {
204→            deleteImportedDeck(intent.data?.path)
205→            reloadIntent.action = action
206→            reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
207→            startActivity(reloadIntent)
208→            finish()
209→        } else {
210→            Timber.i("File import failed")
211→            // Don't import the file if it didn't load properly or doesn't have apkg extension
212→            showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(this, importResult.humanReadableMessage, true)
213→        }
214→    }
215→
216→    private fun deleteImportedDeck(path: String?) {
217→        try {
218→            val file = File(path!!)
219→            val fileUri =
220→                applicationContext?.let {
221→                    FileProvider.getUriForFile(
222→                        it,
223→                        it.applicationContext?.packageName + ".apkgfileprovider",
224→                        File(it.getExternalFilesDir(FileUtil.getDownloadDirectory()), file.name),
225→                    )
226→                }
227→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
228→            contentResolver.delete(fileUri!!, null, null)
229→            Timber.i("onCreate() import successful and downloaded file deleted")
230→        } catch (e: Exception) {
231→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate() import successful and cannot delete file")
232→        }
233→    }
234→
235→    private fun handleImageImport(data: Intent) {
236→        val imageUri =
237→            if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
238→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri::class.java)
239→            } else {
240→                data.data
241→            }
242→
243→        val imageOcclusionIntentBuilder = ImageOcclusionIntentBuilder(this)
244→        val intentImageOcclusion = imageOcclusionIntentBuilder.buildIntent(imageUri)
245→
246→        TaskStackBuilder
247→            .create(this)
248→            .addNextIntentWithParentStack(Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java))
249→            .addNextIntent(intentImageOcclusion)
250→            .startActivities()
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun deleteDownloadedDeck(sharedDeckUri: Uri?) {
254→        if (sharedDeckUri == null) {
255→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded a shared deck but uri was null when trying to delete its file")
256→            return
257→        }
258→
259→        try {
260→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
261→            contentResolver.delete(sharedDeckUri, null, null)
262→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded shared deck deleted")
263→        } catch (e: Exception) {
264→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate: failed to delete downloaded shared deck")
265→        }
266→    }
267→
268→    private fun launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent: Intent) {
269→        // Launcher intents should start DeckPicker if no other task exists,
270→        // otherwise go to previous task
271→        Timber.i("Launching DeckPicker")
272→        reloadIntent.action = Intent.ACTION_MAIN
273→        reloadIntent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_LAUNCHER)
274→        reloadIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
275→        startActivityIfNeeded(reloadIntent, 0)
276→        finish()
277→    }
278→
279→    // COULD_BE_BETTER: Also extract the parameters into here to reduce coupling
280→    @VisibleForTesting
281→    enum class LaunchType {
282→        DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
283→
284→        /** colpkg/apkg/unknown */
285→        FILE_IMPORT,
286→
287→        /** csv/tsv */
288→        TEXT_IMPORT,
289→
290→        /** image */
291→        IMAGE_IMPORT,
292→
293→        SYNC,
294→        REVIEW,
295→        COPY_DEBUG_INFO,
296→    }
297→
298→    companion object {
299→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT = "com.ichi2.anki.COPY_DEBUG_INFO"
300→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA = "clip_data"
301→
302→        private fun isValidViewIntent(intent: Intent): Boolean {
303→            // Negating a negative because we want to call specific attention to the fact that it's invalid
304→            // #6312 - Smart Launcher provided an empty ACTION_VIEW, no point in importing here.
305→            return !isInvalidViewIntent(intent)
306→        }
307→
308→        /** Checks whether storage permissions are granted on the device. If the device is not using legacy storage,
309→         *  it verifies if the app has been granted the necessary storage access permission.
310→         *  @return `true`: if granted, otherwise `false` and shows a missing permission toast
311→         */
312→        fun grantedStoragePermissions(
313→            context: Context,
314→            showToast: Boolean,
315→        ): Boolean {
316→            val granted =
317→                !ScopedStorageService.isLegacyStorage(context) ||
318→                    hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission(context) ||
319→                    Permissions.isExternalStorageManagerCompat()
320→
321→            if (!granted && showToast) {
322→                showThemedToast(context, context.getString(R.string.intent_handler_failed_no_storage_permission), false)
323→            }
324→
325→            return granted
326→        }
327→
328→        @VisibleForTesting
329→        @CheckResult
330→        fun getLaunchType(intent: Intent): LaunchType {
331→            val action = intent.action
332→            return if (action == Intent.ACTION_SEND || Intent.ACTION_VIEW == action && isValidViewIntent(intent)) {
333→                val mimeType = intent.resolveMimeType()
334→                when {
335→                    mimeType?.startsWith("image/") == true -> LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT
336→                    mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
337→                        mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
338→                    else -> LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT
339→                }
340→            } else if ("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC" == action) {
341→                LaunchType.SYNC
342→            } else if (intent.hasExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID)) {
343→                LaunchType.REVIEW
344→            } else if (action == CLIPBOARD_INTENT) {
345→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO
346→            } else {
347→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW
348→            }
349→        }
350→
351→        /**
352→         * Send a Message to AnkiDroidApp so that the DialogMessageHandler forces a sync
353→         */
354→        fun sendDoSyncMsg() {
355→            // Store the message in AnkiDroidApp message holder, which is loaded later in AnkiActivity.onResume
356→            storeMessage(DoSync().toMessage())
357→        }
358→
359→        fun copyStringToClipboardIntent(
360→            context: Context,
361→            textToCopy: String,
362→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).also {
363→            it.action = CLIPBOARD_INTENT
364→            // max length for an intent is 500KB.
365→            // 25000 * 2 (bytes per char) = 50,000 bytes <<< 500KB
366→            it.putExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA, textToCopy.trimToLength(25000))
367→        }
368→
369→        fun requiresCollectionAccess(launchType: LaunchType): Boolean =
370→            when (launchType) {
371→                LaunchType.SYNC,
372→                LaunchType.REVIEW,
373→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
374→                LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT,
375→                LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT,
376→                LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT,
377→                -> true
378→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> false
379→            }
380→
381→        class DoSync :
382→            DialogHandlerMessage(
383→                which = WhichDialogHandler.MSG_DO_SYNC,
384→                analyticName = "DoSyncDialog",
385→            ) {
386→            override fun handleAsyncMessage(activity: AnkiActivity) {
387→                // we may be called via any AnkiActivity but sync is a DeckPicker thing
388→                if (activity !is DeckPicker) {
389→                    showError(
390→                        activity,
391→                        activity.getString(R.string.something_wrong),
392→                        ClassCastException(activity.javaClass.simpleName + " is not " + DeckPicker.javaClass.simpleName),
393→                        true,
394→                    )
395→                    return
396→                }
397→                // let's be clear about the type now that we've checked
398→                val deckPicker = activity
399→
400→                val preferences = deckPicker.sharedPrefs()
401→                val res = deckPicker.resources
402→                val hkey = preferences.getString("hkey", "")
403→                val millisecondsSinceLastSync = millisecondsSinceLastSync(preferences)
404→                val limited = millisecondsSinceLastSync < INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL
405→                if (!limited && hkey!!.isNotEmpty() && NetworkUtils.isOnline) {
406→                    deckPicker.sync()
407→                } else {
408→                    val err = res.getString(R.string.sync_error)
409→                    if (limited) {
410→                        val remainingTimeInSeconds =
411→                            max((INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL - millisecondsSinceLastSync) / 1000, 1)
412→                        // getQuantityString needs an int
413→                        val remaining = min(Int.MAX_VALUE.toLong(), remainingTimeInSeconds).toInt()
414→                        val message =
415→                            res.getQuantityString(
416→                                R.plurals.sync_automatic_sync_needs_more_time,
417→                                remaining,
418→                                remaining,
419→                            )
420→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(err, message, Channel.SYNC)
421→                    } else {
422→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(
423→                            err,
424→                            res.getString(R.string.youre_offline),
425→                            Channel.SYNC,
426→                        )
427→                    }
428→                }
429→                deckPicker.finish()
430→            }
431→
432→            override fun toMessage(): Message = emptyMessage(this.what)
433→
434→            companion object {
435→                const val INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL =
436→                    (
437→                        2 * 60000 // 2min minimum sync interval
438→                    ).toLong()
439→            }
440→        }
441→
442→        /**
443→         * Returns an intent to review a specific deck.
444→         * This does not states which reviewer to use, instead IntentHandler will choose whether to use the
445→         * legacy or the new reviewer based on the "newReviewer" preference.
446→         * It is expected to be used from widget, shortcut, reminders but not from ankidroid directly because of the CLEAR_TOP flag.
447→         */
448→        fun intentToReviewDeckFromShorcuts(
449→            context: Context,
450→            deckId: DeckId,
451→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).apply {
452→            setAction(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
453→            putExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, deckId)
454→        }
455→    }
456→}

 40→@NeedsTest("Selecting APKG does not allow multiple files")
 41→@NeedsTest("Selecting COLPKG does not allow multiple files")
 42→@NeedsTest("Restore backup dialog does not allow multiple files")
 43→class ImportFileSelectionFragment : DialogFragment() {
 44→    override fun onCreateDialog(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): Dialog {
 45→        val entries = buildImportEntries()
 46→        return AlertDialog
 47→            .Builder(requireActivity())
 48→            .title(R.string.menu_import)
 49→            .setItems(
 50→                entries.map { requireActivity().getString(it.titleRes) }.toTypedArray(),
 51→            ) { _, position ->
 52→                val entry = entries[position]
 53→                UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 54→                    UsageAnalytics.Category.LINK_CLICKED,
 55→                    entry.analyticsId,
 56→                )
 57→                openImportFilePicker(
 58→                    activity = requireActivity() as AnkiActivity,
 59→                    fileType = entry.type,
 60→                    multiple = entry.multiple,
 61→                    mimeType = entry.mimeType,
 62→                    extraMimes = entry.extraMimes,
 63→                )
 64→            }.create()
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    private fun buildImportEntries(): List<ImportEntry> {
 68→        return arguments?.let { args ->
 69→            args.classLoader = this@ImportFileSelectionFragment::class.java.classLoader
 70→            val options =
 71→                BundleCompat.getParcelable(args, ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS, ImportOptions::class.java)
 72→                    ?: return emptyList()
 73→            mutableListOf<ImportEntry>().apply {
 74→                if (options.importApkg) {
 75→                    add(
 76→                        ImportEntry(
 77→                            R.string.import_deck_package,
 78→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_APKG_FILE,
 79→                            ImportFileType.APKG,
 80→                        ),
 81→                    )
 82→                }
 83→                if (options.importColpkg) {
 84→                    add(
 85→                        ImportEntry(
 86→                            R.string.import_collection_package,
 87→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_COLPKG_FILE,
 88→                            ImportFileType.COLPKG,
 89→                        ),
 90→                    )
 91→                }
 92→                if (options.importTextFile) {
 93→                    add(
 94→                        ImportEntry(
 95→                            R.string.import_csv,
 96→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_CSV_FILE,
 97→                            ImportFileType.CSV,
 98→                            multiple = false,
 99→                            mimeType = "*/*",
100→                            extraMimes =
101→                                arrayOf(
102→                                    TEXT_PLAIN,
103→                                    "text/comma-separated-values",
104→                                    "text/csv",
105→                                    "text/tab-separated-values",
106→                                ),
107→                        ),
108→                    )
109→                }
110→            }
111→        } ?: emptyList()
112→    }
113→
114→    // safe as this data class is used as a container and it's not involved in any comparing
115→    @Suppress("ArrayInDataClass")
116→    private data class ImportEntry(
117→        @StringRes val titleRes: Int,
118→        val analyticsId: String,
119→        val type: ImportFileType,
120→        var multiple: Boolean = false,
121→        val mimeType: String = "*/*",
122→        val extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
123→    )
124→
125→    @Parcelize
126→    data class ImportOptions(
127→        val importColpkg: Boolean,
128→        val importApkg: Boolean,
129→        val importTextFile: Boolean,
130→    ) : Parcelable
131→
132→    enum class ImportFileType {
133→        APKG,
134→        COLPKG,
135→        CSV,
136→    }
137→
138→    interface ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider {
139→        fun getApkgFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
140→    }
141→
142→    interface CsvImportResultLauncherProvider {
143→        fun getCsvFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
144→    }
145→
146→    companion object {
147→        private const val ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS = "arg_import_options"
148→
149→        fun newInstance(options: ImportOptions) =
150→            ImportFileSelectionFragment().apply {
151→                arguments = bundleOf(ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS to options)
152→            }
153→
154→        /**
155→         * Calls through the system with an [Intent] to pick a file to be imported.
156→         */
157→        fun openImportFilePicker(
158→            activity: AnkiActivity,
159→            fileType: ImportFileType,
160→            multiple: Boolean = false,
161→            mimeType: String = "*/*",
162→            extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
163→        ) {
164→            Timber.d("openImportFilePicker() delegating to file picker intent")
165→            val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_OPEN_DOCUMENT)
166→            intent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_OPENABLE)
167→            intent.type = mimeType
168→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_ADVANCED", true)
169→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.FANCY", true)
170→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_FILESIZE", true)
171→            intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_ALLOW_MULTIPLE, multiple)
172→            extraMimes?.let { intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_MIME_TYPES, it) }
173→
174→            try {
175→                if (
176→                    (fileType == ImportFileType.APKG || fileType == ImportFileType.COLPKG) &&
177→                    activity is ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider
178→                ) {
179→                    activity.getApkgFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
180→                } else if (fileType == ImportFileType.CSV && activity is CsvImportResultLauncherProvider) {
181→                    activity.getCsvFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
182→                } else {
183→                    Timber.w("Activity($activity) can't handle requested import: $fileType")
184→                }
185→            } catch (ex: ActivityNotFoundException) {
186→                Timber.w("No activity to handle openImportFilePicker request")
187→                activity.showSnackbar(R.string.activity_start_failed)
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→}

28→object IntentUtil {
29→    @JvmStatic // (fixable) required due to structure of unit tests
30→    fun canOpenIntent(
31→        context: Context,
32→        intent: Intent,
33→    ): Boolean =
34→        try {
35→            val packageManager = context.packageManager
36→            intent.resolveActivity(packageManager) != null
37→        } catch (e: Exception) {
38→            Timber.w(e)
39→            false
40→        }
41→
42→    fun tryOpenIntent(
43→        activity: AnkiActivity,
44→        intent: Intent,
45→    ) {
46→        try {
47→            if (canOpenIntent(activity, intent)) {
48→                activity.startActivity(intent)
49→            } else {
50→                val errorMsg = activity.getString(R.string.feedback_no_suitable_app_found)
51→                activity.showSnackbar(errorMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
52→            }
53→        } catch (e: Exception) {
54→            Timber.w(e)
55→            val errorMsg = activity.getString(R.string.feedback_no_suitable_app_found)
56→            activity.showSnackbar(errorMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    fun Intent.resolveMimeType(): String? =
61→        if (type == null) {
62→            val extension = MimeTypeMap.getFileExtensionFromUrl(data.toString())
63→            MimeTypeMap.getSingleton().getMimeTypeFromExtension(extension)
64→        } else {
65→            type
66→        }
67→}

conversationHistory.content:
Now I understand the issue! Let me analyze the problem:

## Root Cause Analysis

Looking at the code, I can see the issue:

1. In `IntentHandler.kt`, the `getLaunchType()` function (lines 330-349) determines how to handle incoming intents based on the MIME type.

2. On lines 336-337, it checks for text imports:
```kotlin
mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
```

3. However, **`"text/csv"` is NOT checked**, even though:
   - It's a standard MIME type for CSV files
   - It's included in the file picker's extra MIME types in `ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt` (lines 100-106)

4. When a CSV file with MIME type `"text/csv"` is selected, it falls through to line 338, which treats it as `FILE_IMPORT` (like `.apkg` files) instead of `TEXT_IMPORT`.

## The Fix

I need to add `"text/csv"` to the TEXT_IMPORT condition in `IntentHandler.kt`.

 17→package com.ichi2.anki
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.Intent
 21→import android.net.Uri
 22→import android.os.Bundle
 23→import android.os.Message
 24→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 25→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 26→import androidx.core.app.TaskStackBuilder
 27→import androidx.core.content.FileProvider
 28→import androidx.core.content.IntentCompat
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandler.Companion.storeMessage
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandlerMessage
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.ScopedStorageService
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.services.ReminderService
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.reviewer.ReviewerFragment
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.worker.SyncWorker
 36→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 37→import com.ichi2.libanki.DeckId
 38→import com.ichi2.utils.FileUtil
 39→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.handleFileImport
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.isInvalidViewIntent
 41→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.showImportUnsuccessfulDialog
 42→import com.ichi2.utils.IntentUtil.resolveMimeType
 43→import com.ichi2.utils.NetworkUtils
 44→import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions
 45→import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions.hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission
 46→import com.ichi2.utils.copyToClipboard
 47→import com.ichi2.utils.trimToLength
 48→import timber.log.Timber
 49→import java.io.File
 50→import kotlin.math.max
 51→import kotlin.math.min
 52→
 53→/**
 54→ * Class which handles how the application responds to different intents, forcing it to always be single task,
 55→ * but allowing custom behavior depending on the intent
 56→ * It inherits from [AbstractIntentHandler]
 57→ *
 58→ * @author Tim
 59→ */
 60→class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
 61→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 62→        // Note: This is our entry point from the launcher with intent: android.intent.action.MAIN
 63→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 64→        val intent = intent
 65→        Timber.v(intent.toString())
 66→        val reloadIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
 67→        reloadIntent.setDataAndType(getIntent().data, getIntent().type)
 68→        val action = intent.action
 69→        // #6157 - We want to block actions that need permissions we don't have, but not the default case
 70→        // as this requires nothing
 71→        val runIfStoragePermissions = { runnable: () -> Unit -> performActionIfStorageAccessible(reloadIntent, action) { runnable() } }
 72→        val launchType = getLaunchType(intent)
 73→        // TODO block the UI with some kind of ProgressDialog instead of cancelling the sync work
 74→        if (requiresCollectionAccess(launchType)) {
 75→            SyncWorker.cancel(this)
 76→        }
 77→        when (launchType) {
 78→            LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT ->
 79→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 80→                    handleFileImport(fileIntent, reloadIntent, action)
 81→                    finish()
 82→                }
 83→            LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT ->
 84→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 85→                    onSelectedCsvForImport(fileIntent)
 86→                    finish()
 87→                }
 88→            LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT ->
 89→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 90→                    handleImageImport(intent)
 91→                    finish()
 92→                }
 93→            LaunchType.SYNC -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleSyncIntent(reloadIntent, action) }
 94→            LaunchType.REVIEW -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleReviewIntent(reloadIntent, intent) }
 95→            LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW -> {
 96→                Timber.d("onCreate() performing default action")
 97→                launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
 98→            }
 99→            LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> {
100→                copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent)
101→                finish()
102→            }
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent: Intent) {
107→        Timber.i("Copying debug info to clipboard")
108→        // null string is handled by copyToClipboard in try-catch
109→        this.copyToClipboard(
110→            text = (intent.getStringExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA)!!),
111→            failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info,
112→        )
113→    }
114→
115→    private val fileIntent: Intent
116→        get() {
117→            return if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
118→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Intent::class.java) ?: intent
119→            } else {
120→                intent
121→            }
122→        }
123→
124→    /**
125→     * Execute the runnable if one of the two following conditions are satisfied:
126→     *
127→     *  * AnkiDroid is using an app-private directory to store user data
128→     *  * AnkiDroid is using a legacy directory to store user data but has access to it since storage permission
129→     * has been granted (as long as AnkiDroid targeted API < 30, requested legacy storage, and has not been uninstalled since)
130→     *
131→     */
132→    @NeedsTest("clicking a file in 'Files' to import")
133→    private fun performActionIfStorageAccessible(
134→        reloadIntent: Intent,
135→        action: String?,
136→        block: () -> Unit,
137→    ) {
138→        if (grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {
139→            Timber.i("User has storage permissions. Running intent: %s", action)
140→            block()
141→        } else {
142→            Timber.i("No Storage Permission, cancelling intent '%s'", action)
143→            launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun handleReviewIntent(
148→        reloadIntent: Intent,
149→        reviewerIntent: Intent,
150→    ) {
151→        val deckId = intent.getLongExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, 0)
152→        Timber.i("Handling intent to review deck '%d'", deckId)
153→
154→        val reviewIntent =
155→            if (sharedPrefs().getBoolean("newReviewer", false)) {
156→                ReviewerFragment.getIntent(this)
157→            } else {
158→                Intent(this, Reviewer::class.java).apply {
159→                    addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
160→                }
161→            }
162→        CollectionManager.getColUnsafe().decks.select(deckId)
163→        // Clean the stack out under the reviewer to avoid any incorrect activities / dialogs /
164→        // data state from prior app usage showing after reviewer exits if going to reviewer directly
165→        TaskStackBuilder
166→            .create(applicationContext)
167→            .addNextIntent(reloadIntent)
168→            .addNextIntent(reviewIntent)
169→            .startActivities()
170→        finish()
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun handleSyncIntent(
174→        reloadIntent: Intent,
175→        action: String?,
176→    ) {
177→        Timber.i("Handling Sync Intent")
178→        sendDoSyncMsg()
179→        reloadIntent.action = action
180→        reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
181→        startActivity(reloadIntent)
182→        finish()
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun handleFileImport(
186→        intent: Intent,
187→        reloadIntent: Intent,
188→        action: String?,
189→    ) {
190→        Timber.i("Handling file import")
191→        if (!hasShownAppIntro()) {
192→            Timber.i("Trying to import a file when the app was not started at all")
193→            showThemedToast(this, R.string.app_not_initialized_new, false)
194→            return
195→        }
196→        val importResult = handleFileImport(this, intent)
197→        // attempt to delete the downloaded deck if it is a shared deck download import
198→        if (intent.hasExtra(SharedDecksDownloadFragment.EXTRA_IS_SHARED_DOWNLOAD)) {
199→            deleteDownloadedDeck(intent.data)
200→        }
201→
202→        // Start DeckPicker if we correctly processed ACTION_VIEW
203→        if (importResult.isSuccess) {
204→            deleteImportedDeck(intent.data?.path)
205→            reloadIntent.action = action
206→            reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
207→            startActivity(reloadIntent)
208→            finish()
209→        } else {
210→            Timber.i("File import failed")
211→            // Don't import the file if it didn't load properly or doesn't have apkg extension
212→            showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(this, importResult.humanReadableMessage, true)
213→        }
214→    }
215→
216→    private fun deleteImportedDeck(path: String?) {
217→        try {
218→            val file = File(path!!)
219→            val fileUri =
220→                applicationContext?.let {
221→                    FileProvider.getUriForFile(
222→                        it,
223→                        it.applicationContext?.packageName + ".apkgfileprovider",
224→                        File(it.getExternalFilesDir(FileUtil.getDownloadDirectory()), file.name),
225→                    )
226→                }
227→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
228→            contentResolver.delete(fileUri!!, null, null)
229→            Timber.i("onCreate() import successful and downloaded file deleted")
230→        } catch (e: Exception) {
231→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate() import successful and cannot delete file")
232→        }
233→    }
234→
235→    private fun handleImageImport(data: Intent) {
236→        val imageUri =
237→            if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
238→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri::class.java)
239→            } else {
240→                data.data
241→            }
242→
243→        val imageOcclusionIntentBuilder = ImageOcclusionIntentBuilder(this)
244→        val intentImageOcclusion = imageOcclusionIntentBuilder.buildIntent(imageUri)
245→
246→        TaskStackBuilder
247→            .create(this)
248→            .addNextIntentWithParentStack(Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java))
249→            .addNextIntent(intentImageOcclusion)
250→            .startActivities()
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun deleteDownloadedDeck(sharedDeckUri: Uri?) {
254→        if (sharedDeckUri == null) {
255→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded a shared deck but uri was null when trying to delete its file")
256→            return
257→        }
258→
259→        try {
260→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
261→            contentResolver.delete(sharedDeckUri, null, null)
262→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded shared deck deleted")
263→        } catch (e: Exception) {
264→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate: failed to delete downloaded shared deck")
265→        }
266→    }
267→
268→    private fun launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent: Intent) {
269→        // Launcher intents should start DeckPicker if no other task exists,
270→        // otherwise go to previous task
271→        Timber.i("Launching DeckPicker")
272→        reloadIntent.action = Intent.ACTION_MAIN
273→        reloadIntent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_LAUNCHER)
274→        reloadIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
275→        startActivityIfNeeded(reloadIntent, 0)
276→        finish()
277→    }
278→
279→    // COULD_BE_BETTER: Also extract the parameters into here to reduce coupling
280→    @VisibleForTesting
281→    enum class LaunchType {
282→        DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
283→
284→        /** colpkg/apkg/unknown */
285→        FILE_IMPORT,
286→
287→        /** csv/tsv */
288→        TEXT_IMPORT,
289→
290→        /** image */
291→        IMAGE_IMPORT,
292→
293→        SYNC,
294→        REVIEW,
295→        COPY_DEBUG_INFO,
296→    }
297→
298→    companion object {
299→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT = "com.ichi2.anki.COPY_DEBUG_INFO"
300→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA = "clip_data"
301→
302→        private fun isValidViewIntent(intent: Intent): Boolean {
303→            // Negating a negative because we want to call specific attention to the fact that it's invalid
304→            // #6312 - Smart Launcher provided an empty ACTION_VIEW, no point in importing here.
305→            return !isInvalidViewIntent(intent)
306→        }
307→
308→        /** Checks whether storage permissions are granted on the device. If the device is not using legacy storage,
309→         *  it verifies if the app has been granted the necessary storage access permission.
310→         *  @return `true`: if granted, otherwise `false` and shows a missing permission toast
311→         */
312→        fun grantedStoragePermissions(
313→            context: Context,
314→            showToast: Boolean,
315→        ): Boolean {
316→            val granted =
317→                !ScopedStorageService.isLegacyStorage(context) ||
318→                    hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission(context) ||
319→                    Permissions.isExternalStorageManagerCompat()
320→
321→            if (!granted && showToast) {
322→                showThemedToast(context, context.getString(R.string.intent_handler_failed_no_storage_permission), false)
323→            }
324→
325→            return granted
326→        }
327→
328→        @VisibleForTesting
329→        @CheckResult
330→        fun getLaunchType(intent: Intent): LaunchType {
331→            val action = intent.action
332→            return if (action == Intent.ACTION_SEND || Intent.ACTION_VIEW == action && isValidViewIntent(intent)) {
333→                val mimeType = intent.resolveMimeType()
334→                when {
335→                    mimeType?.startsWith("image/") == true -> LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT
336→                    mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
337→                        mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
338→                    else -> LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT
339→                }
340→            } else if ("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC" == action) {
341→                LaunchType.SYNC
342→            } else if (intent.hasExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID)) {
343→                LaunchType.REVIEW
344→            } else if (action == CLIPBOARD_INTENT) {
345→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO
346→            } else {
347→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW
348→            }
349→        }
350→
351→        /**
352→         * Send a Message to AnkiDroidApp so that the DialogMessageHandler forces a sync
353→         */
354→        fun sendDoSyncMsg() {
355→            // Store the message in AnkiDroidApp message holder, which is loaded later in AnkiActivity.onResume
356→            storeMessage(DoSync().toMessage())
357→        }
358→
359→        fun copyStringToClipboardIntent(
360→            context: Context,
361→            textToCopy: String,
362→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).also {
363→            it.action = CLIPBOARD_INTENT
364→            // max length for an intent is 500KB.
365→            // 25000 * 2 (bytes per char) = 50,000 bytes <<< 500KB
366→            it.putExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA, textToCopy.trimToLength(25000))
367→        }
368→
369→        fun requiresCollectionAccess(launchType: LaunchType): Boolean =
370→            when (launchType) {
371→                LaunchType.SYNC,
372→                LaunchType.REVIEW,
373→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
374→                LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT,
375→                LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT,
376→                LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT,
377→                -> true
378→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> false
379→            }
380→
381→        class DoSync :
382→            DialogHandlerMessage(
383→                which = WhichDialogHandler.MSG_DO_SYNC,
384→                analyticName = "DoSyncDialog",
385→            ) {
386→            override fun handleAsyncMessage(activity: AnkiActivity) {
387→                // we may be called via any AnkiActivity but sync is a DeckPicker thing
388→                if (activity !is DeckPicker) {
389→                    showError(
390→                        activity,
391→                        activity.getString(R.string.something_wrong),
392→                        ClassCastException(activity.javaClass.simpleName + " is not " + DeckPicker.javaClass.simpleName),
393→                        true,
394→                    )
395→                    return
396→                }
397→                // let's be clear about the type now that we've checked
398→                val deckPicker = activity
399→
400→                val preferences = deckPicker.sharedPrefs()
401→                val res = deckPicker.resources
402→                val hkey = preferences.getString("hkey", "")
403→                val millisecondsSinceLastSync = millisecondsSinceLastSync(preferences)
404→                val limited = millisecondsSinceLastSync < INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL
405→                if (!limited && hkey!!.isNotEmpty() && NetworkUtils.isOnline) {
406→                    deckPicker.sync()
407→                } else {
408→                    val err = res.getString(R.string.sync_error)
409→                    if (limited) {
410→                        val remainingTimeInSeconds =
411→                            max((INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL - millisecondsSinceLastSync) / 1000, 1)
412→                        // getQuantityString needs an int
413→                        val remaining = min(Int.MAX_VALUE.toLong(), remainingTimeInSeconds).toInt()
414→                        val message =
415→                            res.getQuantityString(
416→                                R.plurals.sync_automatic_sync_needs_more_time,
417→                                remaining,
418→                                remaining,
419→                            )
420→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(err, message, Channel.SYNC)
421→                    } else {
422→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(
423→                            err,
424→                            res.getString(R.string.youre_offline),
425→                            Channel.SYNC,
426→                        )
427→                    }
428→                }
429→                deckPicker.finish()
430→            }
431→
432→            override fun toMessage(): Message = emptyMessage(this.what)
433→
434→            companion object {
435→                const val INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL =
436→                    (
437→                        2 * 60000 // 2min minimum sync interval
438→                    ).toLong()
439→            }
440→        }
441→
442→        /**
443→         * Returns an intent to review a specific deck.
444→         * This does not states which reviewer to use, instead IntentHandler will choose whether to use the
445→         * legacy or the new reviewer based on the "newReviewer" preference.
446→         * It is expected to be used from widget, shortcut, reminders but not from ankidroid directly because of the CLEAR_TOP flag.
447→         */
448→        fun intentToReviewDeckFromShorcuts(
449→            context: Context,
450→            deckId: DeckId,
451→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).apply {
452→            setAction(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
453→            putExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, deckId)
454→        }
455→    }
456→}

 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.view.MotionEvent
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import android.widget.ArrayAdapter
 22→import android.widget.Spinner
 23→import android.widget.TextView
 24→import androidx.appcompat.app.ActionBar
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck.Companion.fromCollection
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.widgets.DeckDropDownAdapter
 29→import com.ichi2.libanki.*
 30→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.FragmentManagerSupplier
 32→import com.ichi2.utils.asFragmentManagerSupplier
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→
 35→/**
 36→ * Handles expansion of a [Spinner], allowing a press to display a [DeckSelectionDialog]
 37→ * Responsible for filtering the decks to display in the [DeckSelectionDialog]
 38→ *
 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 91→        }
 92→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 93→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 94→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 95→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 96→
 97→                // If this item is selected
 98→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
 99→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
100→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
101→                }
102→
103→                // Return the modified view
104→                return tv
105→            }
106→        }
107→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
108→        setSpinnerListener()
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
113→     */
114→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
115→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
116→    }
117→
118→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
119→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
120→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
121→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
122→            }
123→            true
124→        }
125→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
130→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
131→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
132→     */
133→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
134→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
135→        if (position != -1) {
136→            spinner.setSelection(position)
137→        } else {
138→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
143→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
144→    }
145→
146→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
147→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
148→    }
149→
150→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
151→        spinner.visibility = view
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
156→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
157→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
158→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
159→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
160→     * the current deck id of Collection.
161→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
162→     */
163→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
164→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
165→            selectAllDecks()
166→        } else {
167→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * select in the spinner deck with id
173→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
174→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
175→     * @return whether it was found
176→     */
177→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
178→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
179→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
180→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
181→                spinner.setSelection(position)
182→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
183→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
184→                }
185→                return true
186→            }
187→        }
188→        return false
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
193→     * @return whether selection was a success.
194→     */
195→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
196→        if (!showAllDecks) {
197→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
198→            return false
199→        }
200→        spinner.setSelection(0)
201→        return true
202→    }
203→
204→    /**
205→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
206→     */
207→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
208→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
209→        if (showAllDecks) {
210→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
211→        }
212→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
213→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
214→    }
215→
216→    companion object {
217→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
218→    }
219→}

conversationHistory.content:
Let me analyze this issue carefully. Based on the code and the issue description, the problem appears to be with how the spinner's touch listener is implemented. 

Looking at `DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`, in the `setSpinnerListener()` method (lines 118-126), the touch listener returns `true` for ALL events, which consumes them all. This prevents the spinner's normal dropdown behavior from working.

The issue is that:
1. The touch listener currently consumes ALL touch events (returns `true` for everything)
2. This blocks the spinner's native dropdown from appearing
3. While this works fine for ActionBar spinners (which are custom), it breaks regular spinners like in the note editor

The fix should be to only consume events at the right time or return false when appropriate to allow the spinner's default behavior.

Let me implement the fix:
