 13→ * WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A      *
 14→ * PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.             *
 15→ *                                                                                      *
 16→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with         *
 17→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.                           *
 18→ ****************************************************************************************/
 19→package com.ichi2.libanki
 20→
 21→import androidx.core.text.HtmlCompat
 22→import com.ichi2.libanki.Consts.FIELD_SEPARATOR
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.*
 25→import java.math.BigInteger
 26→import java.security.MessageDigest
 27→import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException
 28→import java.util.*
 29→import java.util.regex.Matcher
 30→import java.util.regex.Pattern
 31→import kotlin.math.*
 32→
 33→// TODO switch to standalone functions and properties and remove Utils container
 34→object Utils {
 35→    // Used to format doubles with English's decimal separator system
 36→    val ENGLISH_LOCALE = Locale("en_US")
 37→
 38→    // Regex pattern used in removing tags from text before diff
 39→    private val commentPattern = Pattern.compile("(?s)<!--.*?-->")
 40→    private val stylePattern = Pattern.compile("(?si)<style.*?>.*?</style>")
 41→    private val scriptPattern = Pattern.compile("(?si)<script.*?>.*?</script>")
 42→    private val tagPattern = Pattern.compile("(?s)<.*?>")
 43→    private val imgPattern = Pattern.compile("(?i)<img[^>]+src=[\"']?([^\"'>]+)[\"']?[^>]*>")
 44→    private val typePattern = Pattern.compile("(?s)\\[\\[type:.+?]]")
 45→    private val avRefPattern = Pattern.compile("(?s)\\[anki:play:.:\\d+?]")
 46→    private val htmlEntitiesPattern = Pattern.compile("&#?\\w+;")
 47→    private const val ALL_CHARACTERS =
 48→        "0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
 49→    private const val BASE91_EXTRA_CHARS = "!#$%&()*+,-./:;<=>?@[]^_`{|}~"
 50→
 51→    /*
 52→     * Locale
 53→     * ***********************************************************************************************
 54→     */
 55→    /*
 56→    * HTML
 57→     * ***********************************************************************************************
 58→     */
 59→    /**
 60→     * Strips a text from <style>...</style>, <script>...</script> and <_any_tag_> HTML tags.
 61→     * @param inputParam The HTML text to be cleaned.
 62→     * @return The text without the aforementioned tags.
 63→     </_any_tag_> */
 64→    fun stripHTML(inputParam: String): String {
 65→        var s = commentPattern.matcher(inputParam).replaceAll("")
 66→        s = stripHTMLScriptAndStyleTags(s)
 67→        s = tagPattern.matcher(s).replaceAll("")
 68→        return entsToTxt(s)
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    /**
 72→     * Strips <style>...</style> and <script>...</script> HTML tags and content from a string.
 73→     * @param inputParam The HTML text to be cleaned.
 74→     * @return The text without the aforementioned tags.
 75→     */
 76→    fun stripHTMLScriptAndStyleTags(inputParam: String): String {
 77→        var htmlMatcher = stylePattern.matcher(inputParam)
 78→        val s = htmlMatcher.replaceAll("")
 79→        htmlMatcher = scriptPattern.matcher(s)
 80→        return htmlMatcher.replaceAll("")
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Strip HTML but keep media filenames
 85→     */
 86→    fun stripHTMLMedia(s: String, replacement: String = " $1 "): String {
 87→        val imgMatcher = imgPattern.matcher(s)
 88→        return stripHTML(imgMatcher.replaceAll(replacement))
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Takes a string and replaces all the HTML symbols in it with their unescaped representation.
 93→     * This should only affect substrings of the form `&something;` and not tags.
 94→     * Internet rumour says that Html.fromHtml() doesn't cover all cases, but it doesn't get less
 95→     * vague than that.
 96→     * @param htmlInput The HTML escaped text
 97→     * @return The text with its HTML entities unescaped.
 98→     */
 99→    // TODO see if method can be refactored to remove the reference to HtmlCompat
100→    private fun entsToTxt(htmlInput: String): String {
101→        // entitydefs defines nbsp as \xa0 instead of a standard space, so we
102→        // replace it first
103→        val html = htmlInput.replace(" ", " ")
104→        val htmlEntities = htmlEntitiesPattern.matcher(html)
105→        val sb = StringBuffer()
106→        while (htmlEntities.find()) {
107→            val spanned =
108→                HtmlCompat.fromHtml(htmlEntities.group(), HtmlCompat.FROM_HTML_MODE_LEGACY)
109→            val replacement = Matcher.quoteReplacement(spanned.toString())
110→            htmlEntities.appendReplacement(sb, replacement)
111→        }
112→        htmlEntities.appendTail(sb)
113→        return sb.toString()
114→    }
115→
116→    /**
117→     * Strip special fields like `[[type:...]]` and `[anki:play...]` from a string.
118→     * @param input The text to be cleaned.
119→     * @return The text without special fields.
120→     */
121→    fun stripSpecialFields(input: String): String {
122→        val s = typePattern.matcher(input).replaceAll("")
123→        return avRefPattern.matcher(s).replaceAll("")
124→    }
125→
126→    /**
127→     * Strip HTML and special fields from a string.
128→     * @param input The text to be cleaned.
129→     * @return The text without HTML and special fields.
130→     */
131→    fun stripHTMLAndSpecialFields(input: String): String {
132→        val s = stripHTML(input)
133→        return stripSpecialFields(s)
134→    }
135→
136→    /*
137→     * IDs
138→     * ***********************************************************************************************
139→     */
140→    /** Given a list of integers, return a string '(int1,int2,...)'.  */
141→    fun ids2str(ids: IntArray?): String = StringBuilder().apply {
142→        append("(")
143→        if (ids != null) {
144→            val s = Arrays.toString(ids)
145→            append(s.substring(1, s.length - 1))
146→        }
147→        append(")")
148→    }.toString()
149→
150→    /** Given a list of integers, return a string '(int1,int2,...)'.  */
151→    fun ids2str(ids: LongArray?): String = StringBuilder().apply {
152→        append("(")
153→        if (ids != null) {
154→            val s = Arrays.toString(ids)
155→            append(s.substring(1, s.length - 1))
156→        }
157→        append(")")
158→    }.toString()
159→
160→    /** Given a list of integers, return a string '(int1,int2,...)', in order given by the iterator.  */
161→    fun <T> ids2str(ids: Iterable<T>): String = StringBuilder(512).apply {
162→        append("(")
163→        for ((index, id) in ids.withIndex()) {
164→            if (index != 0) {
165→                append(", ")
166→            }
167→            append(id)
168→        }
169→        append(")")
170→    }.toString()
171→
172→    // used in ankiweb
173→    private fun base62(numParam: Int, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") extra: String): String {
174→        var num = numParam
175→        val table = ALL_CHARACTERS + extra
176→        val len = table.length
177→        var buf = ""
178→        var mod: Int
179→        while (num != 0) {
180→            mod = num % len
181→            buf += table.substring(mod, mod + 1)
182→            num /= len
183→        }
184→        return buf
185→    }
186→
187→    // all printable characters minus quotes, backslash and separators
188→    private fun base91(num: Int): String {
189→        return base62(num, BASE91_EXTRA_CHARS)
190→    }
191→
192→    /** return a base91-encoded 64bit random number  */
193→    fun guid64(): String {
194→        return base91(
195→            Random().nextInt((2.0.pow(61.0) - 1).toInt())
196→        )
197→    }
198→
199→    /**
200→     * Fields
201→     * ***********************************************************************************************
202→     */
203→    fun joinFields(list: Array<String>): String {
204→        val result = StringBuilder(128)
205→        for (i in 0 until list.size - 1) {
206→            result.append(list[i]).append("\u001f")
207→        }
208→        if (list.isNotEmpty()) {
209→            result.append(list[list.size - 1])
210→        }
211→        return result.toString()
212→    }
213→
214→    // TODO ensure manual conversion is correct
215→    fun splitFields(fields: String): MutableList<String> {
216→        // -1 ensures that we don't drop empty fields at the ends
217→        return fields.split(FIELD_SEPARATOR).toMutableList()
218→    }
219→
220→    /*
221→     * Checksums
222→     * ***********************************************************************************************
223→     */
224→    /**
225→     * SHA1 checksum.
226→     * Equivalent to python sha1.hexdigest()
227→     *
228→     * @param data the string to generate hash from
229→     * @return A string of length 40 containing the hexadecimal representation of the MD5 checksum of data.
230→     */
231→    fun checksum(data: String?): String {
232→        if (data == null) {
233→            return ""
234→        }
235→        val md: MessageDigest
236→        var digest: ByteArray? = null
237→        try {
238→            md = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA1")
239→            digest = md.digest(data.toByteArray(charset("UTF-8")))
240→        } catch (e: NoSuchAlgorithmException) {
241→            Timber.e(e, "Utils.checksum: No such algorithm.")
242→            throw RuntimeException(e)
243→        } catch (e: UnsupportedEncodingException) {
244→            Timber.e(e, "Utils.checksum :: UnsupportedEncodingException")
245→        }
246→        val biginteger = BigInteger(1, digest)
247→        var result = biginteger.toString(16)
248→
249→        // pad with zeros to length of 40 This method used to pad
250→        // to the length of 32. As it turns out, sha1 has a digest
251→        // size of 160 bits, leading to a hex digest size of 40,
252→        // not 32.
253→        if (result.length < 40) {
254→            val zeroes = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
255→            result = zeroes.substring(0, zeroes.length - result.length) + result
256→        }
257→        return result
258→    }
259→
260→    /**
261→     * Optimized in case of sortIdx = 0
262→     * @param fields Fields of a note
263→     * @param sortIdx An index of the field
264→     * @return The field at sortIdx, without html media, and the csum of the first field.
265→     */
266→    fun sfieldAndCsum(fields: List<String>, sortIdx: Int): Pair<String, Long> {
267→        val firstStripped = stripHTMLMedia(fields[0])
268→        val sortStripped = if (sortIdx == 0) firstStripped else stripHTMLMedia(fields[sortIdx])
269→        return Pair(sortStripped, fieldChecksumWithoutHtmlMedia(firstStripped))
270→    }
271→
272→    /**
273→     * @param data the string to generate hash from. Html media should be removed
274→     * @return 32 bit unsigned number from first 8 digits of sha1 hash
275→     */
276→    private fun fieldChecksumWithoutHtmlMedia(data: String?): Long {
277→        return java.lang.Long.valueOf(checksum(data).substring(0, 8), 16)
278→    }
279→}

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.173 (commit: 93f4312a).

## Issue to Resolve

Add `Show play buttons on cards with audio` preference as in Anki Desktop
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Anki Desktop has a feature called  `Show play buttons on cards with audio`, but AnkiDroid doesn't have it

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add the pref to AnkiDroid


**Additional context**
![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/69634269/37388d7f-7313-4ce6-86f9-9f04d228dddf)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aankitects%2Fanki%20hide_audio_play_buttons&type=code

https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/e327195470824074465657f98bb0282c7c93a56e/qt/aqt/main.py#L587-L592

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`

- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt`

- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.
I'll analyze this issue and implement the fix to add the "Show play buttons on cards with audio" preference to AnkiDroid. Let me start by examining the relevant files.

1→/*

  6→ *  Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later

  8→ *
  9→ *  This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY
 10→ *  WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A
 11→ *  PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
 12→ *
 13→ *  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15→ */
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 26→import timber.log.Timber
 27→import java.io.BufferedReader
 28→import java.io.IOException
 29→import java.io.InputStream
 30→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 31→
 32→class HtmlGenerator(
 33→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 34→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 35→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 36→    val resources: Resources
 37→) {
 38→
 39→    @CheckResult
 40→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 41→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 45→        return when (side) {
 46→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 47→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 48→        }
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 52→        return Sound.expandSounds(content)
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    companion object {
 56→        fun createInstance(
 57→            context: Context,
 58→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 59→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 60→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 61→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 62→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 63→
 64→            return HtmlGenerator(
 65→                typeAnswer,
 66→                cardAppearance,
 67→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 68→                context.resources
 69→            )
 70→        }
 71→
 72→        /**
 73→         * Load the template for the card
 74→         */
 75→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 76→            try {
 77→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 78→                return CardTemplate(data)
 79→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 80→                Timber.w(e)
 81→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        /**
 86→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 87→         *
 88→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 89→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 90→         */
 91→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
 92→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
 93→            try {
 94→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
 95→                var line: String?
 96→                val sb = StringBuilder()
 97→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
 98→                    sb.append(line)
 99→                }
100→                rd.close()
101→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
102→            } catch (e: Exception) {
103→                Timber.w(e)
104→            }
105→            return contentOfMyInputStream
106→        }
107→    }
108→}
109→

220→        /**
221→         * expandSounds takes content with embedded sound file placeholders and expands them to reference the actual media
222→         * file
223→         *
224→         * @param content -- card content to be rendered that may contain embedded audio
225→         * @return -- the same content but in a format that will render working play buttons when audio was embedded
226→         */
227→        fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
228→            return addPlayIcons(content)
229→        }
230→
231→        /**
232→         * @param soundDir -- base path to the media files.
233→         * @param sound -- path to the sound file from the card content.
234→         * @return absolute URI to the sound file.
235→         */
236→        fun getSoundPath(soundDir: String, sound: String): String {
237→            val trimmedSound = sound.trim { it <= ' ' }
238→            return if (hasURIScheme(trimmedSound)) {
239→                trimmedSound
240→            } else {
241→                soundDir + Uri.encode(sound.trimEnd())
242→            }
243→        }
244→
245→        /**
246→         * @param path -- path to the sound file from the card content.
247→         * @return true if path is well-formed URI and contains URI scheme.
248→         */
249→        private fun hasURIScheme(path: String): Boolean {
250→            val uriMatcher = sUriPattern.matcher(path.trim { it <= ' ' })
251→            return uriMatcher.matches() && uriMatcher.group(2) != null
252→        }
253→    }
254→}

conversationHistory.content:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 05a5e229).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: Edit Note Preview does not show the expected card
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

open the browser
Select a card which is not card 1 of the note
long press
in the menu, tap "edit note"
Tap preview

### Expected behaviour

You should see the selected card in the preview mode (or at least, the card should be consistent with the card underlined at the bottom of the editor)

### Actual behaviour

First card of the note is shown (At least I assume. In the case I was interested in, I saw the "this card is empty" warning)

### Debug info

```text
2.17 alpha 3
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This will be slightly easier to fix once https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/14719 goes in:

* `ordinal` is provided to the editor: 
* `ordinal` is `ord`, NOT the index into the array
  * `{{c1::AA}} {{c3::BB}}` returns the second ordinal as 2

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L84

`mOrdinal` is not used to set `templateIndex` in this branch:

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L233-L241

`setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle` uses `templateIndex = 0` and displays the wrong card

I've added two regression tests to help guide a fix

One undefined question: what should the note editor previewer show if one of the cards is empty? Should it be skipped, or displayed

Should this be different between:
* Card Template Preview
* Note Editor Preview
* Card Browser Preview (less likely to want to see empty cards)

For when a few dependencies have been merged:

```kotlin
    private fun indexFromOrdinal(col: Collection, ordinal: Int): Int {
        return when (mEditedNotetype?.isCloze) {
            true -> col.clozeNumbersInNote(col.newNote(mEditedNotetype!!)).indexOf(mOrdinal)
            else -> ordinal
        }
    }
```
Is that correct? with c1 and c3, that will return [1,3], and ord 3 will be an index out of bounds, won't it?

Edit: sorry, ignore me - I mistook this for numbers[ord] instead of searching for the given value.
In the note editor preview, there seems to be no specific card. So I don’t understand what sense it would make to have this behavior on the note editor preview.

For card template preview, I certainly believe you should see the preview for the card you are looking it.

For the browser, if the card selected in the browser is empty, I believe you should show that the card is empty. That’s an information I certainly may want to have if it is the case. Because either:
* there was no recent "clean card"
* there was a clear card, and this is card 1, generated in order to ensure the note is not deleted. 

The first case may be interesting, the second is certainly something that should be noted.

Also, if we touch this part of the code, it may be worth to give more context, by stating either:
* This card is empty. The n cards of this note are empty.
* This card is empty. Jump to the first non-empty card. (with a link/button)

And I guess, potentially, a checkbox to skip the empty card when browsing which cards will be generated.


If you agree it makes sense, I assume it should be many different bugs so they can be easily tackled

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.
I'll analyze this issue and implement a fix. Let me start by examining the relevant code to understand the problem.

263→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
264→     *
265→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
266→     */
267→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
268→    fun modSchema() {
269→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
270→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
271→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
272→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
273→             code to handle the user's choice */
274→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
275→        }
276→        modSchemaNoCheck()
277→    }

434→  GROUP BY nid

462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

548→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
549→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
550→        // cards without notes
551→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
555→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
556→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
557→        ) > 0
558→        // notes without cards or models
559→        if (badNotes) {
560→            return false
561→        }
562→        // invalid ords
563→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
564→            // ignore clozes
565→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
566→                continue
567→            }
568→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
569→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
570→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
571→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
572→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
573→                tmpls.length(),
574→                m.getLong("id")
575→            ) > 0
576→            if (badOrd) {
577→                return false
578→            }
579→        }
580→        return true
581→    }

583→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
584→        if (!debugLog) return
585→
586→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
587→
588→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
589→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
590→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
591→
592→        val objectsString = objects
593→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
594→            .joinToString(", ")
595→
596→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
597→    }
598→
599→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
600→        mLogHnd?.let {
601→            try {
602→                it.println(s)
603→            } catch (e: Exception) {
604→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
605→            }
606→        }
607→        Timber.d(s)
608→    }
609→
610→    private fun _openLog() {
611→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
612→        if (!debugLog) {
613→            return
614→        }
615→        try {
616→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
617→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
618→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
619→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
620→                    lpath2.delete()
621→                }
622→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
623→            }
624→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
625→        } catch (e: IOException) {
626→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
627→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
628→            debugLog = false
629→        }
630→    }
631→
632→    private fun _closeLog() {
633→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
634→        mLogHnd?.close()
635→        mLogHnd = null
636→    }

 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.content.Intent
 19→import android.os.Bundle
 20→import android.view.View
 21→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.NoteService
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.NoteService.getFieldsAsBundleForPreview
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.NotetypeJson
 26→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 27→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 28→import com.ichi2.utils.stringIterable
 29→import org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers.*
 30→import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
 31→import org.junit.Assert
 32→import org.junit.Ignore
 33→import org.junit.Test
 34→import org.junit.jupiter.api.assertThrows
 35→import org.junit.runner.RunWith
 36→import org.robolectric.Robolectric
 37→import org.robolectric.RobolectricTestRunner
 38→import org.robolectric.annotation.Config
 39→
 40→@RunWith(RobolectricTestRunner::class)
 41→class CardTemplatePreviewerTest : RobolectricTest() {
 42→
 43→    @Test
 44→    fun testPreviewUnsavedTemplate() {
 45→        val modelName = "Basic"
 46→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
 47→        val template = collectionBasicModelOriginal.getJSONArray("tmpls").getJSONObject(0)
 48→        template.put("qfmt", template.getString("qfmt") + "PREVIEWER_TEST")
 49→        val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, collectionBasicModelOriginal)
 50→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 51→        intent.putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
 52→        intent.putExtra("index", 0)
 53→
 54→        var previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 55→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
 56→        var testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
 57→        Assert.assertTrue(
 58→            "model change did not show up?",
 59→            testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.q().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST") &&
 60→                testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.a().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST")
 61→        )
 62→
 63→        // Take it through a destroy/re-create lifecycle in order to test instance state persistence
 64→        val outBundle = Bundle()
 65→        previewerController.saveInstanceState(outBundle)
 66→        previewerController.pause().stop().destroy()
 67→        previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java).create(outBundle).start().resume().visible()
 68→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
 69→        testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
 70→        Assert.assertTrue(
 71→            "model change not preserved in lifecycle??",
 72→            testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.q().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST") &&
 73→                testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.a().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST")
 74→        )
 75→
 76→        // Make sure we can click
 77→        Assert.assertFalse("Showing the answer already?", testCardTemplatePreviewer.showingAnswer)
 78→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.disableDoubleClickPrevention()
 79→        val showAnswerButton = testCardTemplatePreviewer.findViewById<View>(R.id.preview_buttons_layout)
 80→        showAnswerButton.performClick()
 81→        Assert.assertTrue("Not showing the answer?", testCardTemplatePreviewer.showingAnswer)
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    @Test
 85→    fun testPreviewUnsavedTemplate_Basic() {
 86→        val modelName = "Basic"
 87→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
 88→        val fields = collectionBasicModelOriginal.fieldsNames
 89→        val template = collectionBasicModelOriginal.getJSONArray("tmpls").getJSONObject(0)
 90→        template.put("qfmt", template.getString("qfmt") + "PREVIEWER_TEST")
 91→        val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, collectionBasicModelOriginal)
 92→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 93→        intent.putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
 94→        intent.putExtra("index", 0)
 95→
 96→        val previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 97→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
 98→        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
 99→        val arr = testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.note().fields
100→        assertThat(arr[0], equalTo("(" + fields[0] + ")"))
101→        assertThat(arr[1], equalTo("(" + fields[1] + ")"))
102→    }
103→
104→    @Test
105→    @Config(qualifiers = "en")
106→    fun testPreviewUnsavedTemplate_Cloze() {
107→        val modelName = "Cloze"
108→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
109→        val fields = collectionBasicModelOriginal.fieldsNames
110→        val template = collectionBasicModelOriginal.getJSONArray("tmpls").getJSONObject(0)
111→        template.put("qfmt", template.getString("qfmt") + "PREVIEWER_TEST")
112→        val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, collectionBasicModelOriginal)
113→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
114→        intent.putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
115→        intent.putExtra("index", 0)
116→
117→        val previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
118→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
119→        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
120→        val arr = testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.note().fields
121→        assertThat(arr[0], equalTo(testCardTemplatePreviewer.getString(R.string.cloze_sample_text, "c1")))
122→        assertThat(arr[1], equalTo("(" + fields[1] + ")"))
123→    }
124→
125→    @Test
126→    @Config(qualifiers = "en")
127→    fun testPreviewUnsavedTemplate_basic_answer() {
128→        val modelName = "Basic (type in the answer)"
129→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
130→        val fields = collectionBasicModelOriginal.fieldsNames
131→        val template = collectionBasicModelOriginal.getJSONArray("tmpls").getJSONObject(0)
132→        template.put("qfmt", template.getString("qfmt") + "PREVIEWER_TEST")
133→        val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, collectionBasicModelOriginal)
134→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
135→        intent.putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
136→        intent.putExtra("index", 0)
137→
138→        val previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
139→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
140→        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
141→        val arr = testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.note().fields
142→        assertThat(arr[0], equalTo("(" + fields[0] + ")"))
143→        assertThat(arr[1], equalTo(testCardTemplatePreviewer.getString(R.string.basic_answer_sample_text)))
144→    }
145→
146→    @Test
147→    fun testPreviewNormal() {
148→        // Make sure we test previewing a new card template
149→        val modelName = "Basic (and reversed card)"
150→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
151→        val testCard1 = getSavedCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)
152→        val testCard2 = getSavedCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 1)
153→
154→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
155→        intent.putExtra("cardList", longArrayOf(testCard1.id, testCard2.id))
156→        intent.putExtra("index", 0)
157→
158→        var previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
159→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
160→
161→        // Take it through a destroy/re-create lifecycle in order to test instance state persistence
162→        val outBundle = Bundle()
163→        previewerController.saveInstanceState(outBundle)
164→        previewerController.pause().stop().destroy()
165→        previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java).create(outBundle).start().resume().visible()
166→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
167→        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
168→
169→        // Make sure we can click
170→        Assert.assertFalse("Showing the answer already?", testCardTemplatePreviewer.showingAnswer)
171→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.disableDoubleClickPrevention()
172→        val showAnswerButton = testCardTemplatePreviewer.findViewById<View>(R.id.preview_buttons_layout)
173→        showAnswerButton.performClick()
174→        Assert.assertTrue("Not showing the answer?", testCardTemplatePreviewer.showingAnswer)
175→    }
176→
177→    @Test
178→    fun testPreviewNoteEditorFieldData() {
179→        val cloze = "Cloze"
180→        val basicAndReverseOptional = "Basic (optional reversed card)"
181→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(cloze)
182→        val basicAndReverseOptionalModel = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(basicAndReverseOptional)
183→        val cardsList = ArrayList<String>()
184→        val tmpls = collectionBasicModelOriginal.getJSONArray("tmpls")
185→        for (name in tmpls.stringIterable()) {
186→            cardsList.add(name)
187→        }
188→        Assert.assertTrue(cardsList.size == 1)
189→        cardsList.clear()
190→        val tmplsBR = basicAndReverseOptionalModel.getJSONArray("tmpls")
191→        for (name in tmplsBR.stringIterable()) {
192→            cardsList.add(name)
193→        }
194→        Assert.assertTrue(cardsList.size == 2)
195→    }
196→

conversationHistory.content: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: a854e93b). ## Issue to Resolve [Bug] AnkiDroidJS API returns incorrect data type ###### Reproduction Steps 1. Add a new basic type note. 2. Add the following code in the front template of the note: ```html api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: {{Front}} ``` 3. Study the new card in AnkiDroid. ###### Expected Result According to the `AnkiDroid Javascript API wiki`, the return value type of api.ankiGetCardQueue() is [`int`](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/AnkiDroid-Javascript-API#type-of-return-value-17). Therefore, it should display `api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: int` on the front of the card. ###### Actual Result It displays `api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: string` in the front of the card. ###### Debug info ``` AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 10 (SDK 29) ProductFlavor = full Manufacturer = HUAWEI Model = MXW-AN00 Hardware = mt6873 Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 10; MXW-AN00 Build/HONORMXW-AN00; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/92.0.4515.105 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = 82400b65-bd87-4883-a1f6-b911150f725b Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ###### Research *Enter an [x] character to confirm the points below:* - [x] I have read the [support page](https://ankidroid.org/docs/help.html) and am reporting a bug or enhancement request specific to AnkiDroid - [x] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [x] I have searched for similar existing issues here and on the user forum - [x] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Based on the AnkiDroidJS API wiki, almost every API that was expected to return data of type int, long, or boolean, is actually returning data of type string. I will push the fix which return correct data types. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.
I'll analyze this issue and implement a fix. Let me start by examining the relevant file to understand the problem.

 20→package com.ichi2.anki
 21→
 22→import android.content.Context
 23→import android.content.Intent
 24→import android.net.Uri
 25→import androidx.lifecycle.lifecycleScope
 26→import com.github.zafarkhaja.semver.Version
 27→import com.google.android.material.snackbar.Snackbar
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeBuryCard
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeBuryNote
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeError
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeFlagCard
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeMarkCard
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeSetDue
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendCard
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendNote
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.flagCommands
 37→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.model.CardsOrNotes
 39→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.rescheduleCards
 40→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.resetCards
 41→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.setMaxLines
 42→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 43→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 44→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 45→import com.ichi2.libanki.Decks
 46→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
 47→import com.ichi2.utils.NetworkUtils
 48→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 49→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
 50→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
 51→import kotlinx.serialization.builtins.ListSerializer
 52→import kotlinx.serialization.builtins.serializer
 53→import kotlinx.serialization.json.Json
 54→import org.json.JSONException
 55→import org.json.JSONObject
 56→import timber.log.Timber
 57→
 58→open class AnkiDroidJsAPI(private val activity: AbstractFlashcardViewer) {
 59→    private val currentCard: Card
 60→        get() = activity.currentCard!!
 61→
 62→    private val getColUnsafe: Collection
 63→        get() = activity.getColUnsafe
 64→
 65→    /**
 66→     Javascript Interface class for calling Java function from AnkiDroid WebView
 67→     see js-api.js for available functions
 68→     */
 69→
 70→    private val context: Context = activity
 71→
 72→    // Text to speech
 73→    private val talker = JavaScriptTTS()
 74→
 75→    // Speech to Text
 76→    private val speechRecognizer = JavaScriptSTT(context)
 77→
 78→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, boolean: Boolean): ByteArray {
 79→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, boolean.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, int: Int): ByteArray {
 83→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, int.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, long: Long): ByteArray {
 87→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, long.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, string: String): ByteArray {
 91→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, string).toString().toByteArray()
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    /**
 95→     * The method parse json data and return api contract object
 96→     * @param byteArray
 97→     * @return apiContract or null
 98→     */
 99→    private fun parseJsApiContract(byteArray: ByteArray): ApiContract? {
100→        try {
101→            val data = JSONObject(byteArray.decodeToString())
102→            val cardSuppliedApiVersion = data.optString("version", "")
103→            val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact = data.optString("developer", "")
104→            val cardSuppliedData = data.optString("data", "")
105→            val isValid = requireApiVersion(cardSuppliedApiVersion, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
106→            return ApiContract(isValid, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact, cardSuppliedData)
107→        } catch (j: JSONException) {
108→            Timber.w(j)
109→            activity.runOnUiThread {
110→                activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, j.localizedMessage))
111→            }
112→        }
113→        return null
114→    }
115→
116→    /*
117→     * see 02-strings.xml
118→     * Show Error code when mark card or flag card unsupported
119→     * 1 - mark card
120→     * 2 - flag card
121→     *
122→     * show developer contact if js api used in card is deprecated
123→     */
124→    private fun showDeveloperContact(errorCode: Int, apiDevContact: String) {
125→        val errorMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.anki_js_error_code, errorCode)
126→        val snackbarMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.api_version_developer_contact, apiDevContact, errorMsg)
127→
128→        activity.showSnackbar(snackbarMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE) {
129→            setMaxLines(3)
130→            setAction(R.string.reviewer_invalid_api_version_visit_documentation) {
131→                activity.openUrl(Uri.parse("https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki"))
132→            }
133→        }
134→    }
135→
136→    /**
137→     * Supplied api version must be equal to current api version to call mark card, toggle flag functions etc.
138→     */
139→    private fun requireApiVersion(apiVer: String, apiDevContact: String): Boolean {
140→        try {
141→            if (apiDevContact.isEmpty() || apiVer.isEmpty()) {
142→                activity.runOnUiThread {
143→                    activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, ""))
144→                }
145→                return false
146→            }
147→            val versionCurrent = Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.sCurrentJsApiVersion)
148→            val versionSupplied = Version.parse(apiVer)
149→
150→            /*
151→            * if api major version equals to supplied major version then return true and also check for minor version and patch version
152→            * show toast for update and contact developer if need updates
153→            * otherwise return false
154→            */
155→            return when {
156→                versionSupplied == versionCurrent -> {
157→                    true
158→                }
159→                versionSupplied.isLowerThan(versionCurrent) -> {
160→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
161→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.update_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
162→                    }
163→                    versionSupplied.isHigherThanOrEquivalentTo(Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.sMinimumJsApiVersion))
164→                }
165→                else -> {
166→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
167→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.valid_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
168→                    }
169→                    false
170→                }
171→            }
172→        } catch (e: Exception) {
173→            Timber.w(e, "requireApiVersion::exception")
174→        }
175→        return false
176→    }
177→
178→    /**
179→     * Handle js api request,
180→     * some of the methods are overridden in Reviewer.kt and default values are returned.
181→     * @param methodName
182→     * @param bytes
183→     * @param returnDefaultValues `true` if default values should be returned (if non-[Reviewer])
184→     * @return
185→     */
186→    open suspend fun handleJsApiRequest(methodName: String, bytes: ByteArray, returnDefaultValues: Boolean = true) = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
187→        // the method will call to set the card supplied data and is valid version for each api request
188→        val apiContract = parseJsApiContract(bytes)!!
189→        // if api not init or is api not called from reviewer then return default -1
190→        // also other action will not be modified
191→        if (!apiContract.isValid or returnDefaultValues) {
192→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, -1)
193→        }
194→
195→        val cardDataForJsAPI = activity.getCardDataForJsApi()
196→        val apiParams = apiContract.cardSuppliedData
197→
198→        return@withContext when (methodName) {
199→            "init" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
200→            "newCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.newCardCount)
201→            "lrnCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.lrnCardCount)
202→            "revCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.revCardCount)
203→            "eta" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.eta)
204→            "nextTime1" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime1)
205→            "nextTime2" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime2)
206→            "nextTime3" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime3)
207→            "nextTime4" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime4)
208→            "toggleFlag" -> {
209→                if (apiParams !in flagCommands) {
210→                    showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeFlagCard, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
211→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
212→                }
213→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.executeCommand(flagCommands[apiParams]!!))
214→            }
215→            "markCard" -> processAction({ activity.executeCommand(ViewerCommand.MARK) }, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeMarkCard, ::convertToByteArray)
216→            "buryCard" -> processAction(activity::buryCard, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeBuryCard, ::convertToByteArray)
217→            "buryNote" -> processAction(activity::buryNote, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeBuryNote, ::convertToByteArray)
218→            "suspendCard" -> processAction(activity::suspendCard, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendCard, ::convertToByteArray)
219→            "suspendNote" -> processAction(activity::suspendNote, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendNote, ::convertToByteArray)
220→            "setCardDue" -> {
221→                try {
222→                    val days = apiParams.toInt()

387→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)

389→        val searchResult: MutableList<String> = ArrayList()
390→        for (s in cards) {

393→            val fieldsName = s.card.model(getColUnsafe).fieldsNames
394→
395→            val noteId = s.card.nid
396→            val cardId = s.card.id
397→            jsonObject.put("cardId", cardId)
398→            jsonObject.put("noteId", noteId)
399→
400→            val jsonFieldObject = JSONObject()
401→            fieldsName.zip(fieldsData).forEach { pair ->
402→                jsonFieldObject.put(pair.component1(), pair.component2())
403→            }
404→            jsonObject.put("fieldsData", jsonFieldObject)
405→
406→            searchResult.add(jsonObject.toString())
407→        }
408→
409→        // quote result to prevent JSON injection attack
410→        val jsonEncodedString = JSONObject.quote(searchResult.toString())
411→        activity.runOnUiThread {
412→            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSearchCard($jsonEncodedString)", null)
413→        }
414→        convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
415→    }

Now I can see the issue! The problem is in the `convertToByteArray` methods. They all convert values to strings using `.toString()` before storing them in `ApiResult`, which then outputs JSON where all values are strings.

Let me check how ApiResult is used and understand the structure better:

conversationHistory.name: read

conversationHistory.content:
Perfect! Now I understand the issue. The problem is that all `convertToByteArray` methods convert their values to strings before passing to `ApiResult`, which then stores them as strings in the JSON. This causes the JavaScript API to receive all values as strings instead of the correct data types (int, long, boolean, string).

The fix is to modify `ApiResult` to handle different data types properly and store them as their actual types in the JSON, not as string representations.

Let me implement the fix:

 54→class CardBrowserViewModel(
 55→    preferences: SharedPreferencesProvider
 56→) : ViewModel(), SharedPreferencesProvider by preferences {
 57→    val cards = CardBrowser.CardCollection<CardBrowser.CardCache>()
 58→
 59→    /** The CardIds of all the cards in the results */
 60→    val allCardIds get() = cards.map { c -> c.id }.toLongArray()
 61→
 62→    var searchTerms: String = ""
 63→    var restrictOnDeck: String = ""
 64→    var currentFlag = 0
 65→
 66→    /**
 67→     * Whether the browser is working in Cards mode or Notes mode.
 68→     * default: [CARDS]
 69→     * */
 70→    val cardsOrNotesFlow = MutableStateFlow(CARDS)
 71→    val cardsOrNotes get() = cardsOrNotesFlow.value
 72→
 73→    // card that was clicked (not marked)
 74→    var currentCardId: CardId = 0
 75→    var order = SortType.NO_SORTING
 76→    var orderAsc = false
 77→
 78→    val column1IndexFlow = MutableStateFlow(sharedPrefs().getInt(DISPLAY_COLUMN_1_KEY, 0))
 79→    val column2IndexFlow = MutableStateFlow(sharedPrefs().getInt(DISPLAY_COLUMN_2_KEY, 0))
 80→    val column1Index get() = column1IndexFlow.value
 81→    val column2Index get() = column2IndexFlow.value
 82→
 83→    /** The query which is currently in the search box, potentially null. Only set when search box was open  */
 84→    var tempSearchQuery: String? = null
 85→
 86→    var isInMultiSelectMode = false
 87→
 88→    val isTruncatedFlow: MutableStateFlow<Boolean> =
 89→        MutableStateFlow(sharedPrefs().getBoolean("isTruncated", false))
 90→    val isTruncated get() = isTruncatedFlow.value
 91→
 92→    private val _selectedRows: MutableSet<CardBrowser.CardCache> = Collections.synchronizedSet(LinkedHashSet())
 93→
 94→    // immutable accessor for _selectedRows
 95→    val selectedRows: Set<CardBrowser.CardCache> get() = _selectedRows
 96→
 97→    private val refreshSelectedRowsFlow = MutableSharedFlow<Unit>()
 98→    val selectedRowsFlow: Flow<Set<CardBrowser.CardCache>> =
 99→        flowOf(selectedRows).combine(refreshSelectedRowsFlow) { row, _ -> row }
100→
101→    val selectedCardIds: List<Long>
102→        get() = selectedRows.map { c -> c.id }
103→    var lastSelectedPosition = 0
104→
105→    val cardInfoDestination: CardInfoDestination?
106→        get() {
107→            val firstSelectedCard = selectedCardIds.firstOrNull() ?: return null
108→            return CardInfoDestination(firstSelectedCard)
109→        }
110→
111→    init {
112→        column1IndexFlow
113→            .onEach { index -> sharedPrefs().edit { putInt(DISPLAY_COLUMN_1_KEY, index) } }
114→            .launchIn(viewModelScope)
115→
116→        column2IndexFlow
117→            .onEach { index -> sharedPrefs().edit { putInt(DISPLAY_COLUMN_2_KEY, index) } }
118→            .launchIn(viewModelScope)
119→
120→        viewModelScope.launch {
121→            val cardsOrNotes = withCol { CardsOrNotes.fromCollection(this) }
122→            cardsOrNotesFlow.update { cardsOrNotes }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    /** Whether any rows are selected */
127→    fun hasSelectedAnyRows(): Boolean = selectedRows.isNotEmpty()
128→
129→    /**
130→     * All the notes of the selected cards will be marked
131→     * If one or more card is unmarked, all will be marked,
132→     * otherwise, they will be unmarked
133→     */
134→    suspend fun toggleMark(cardIds: List<CardId>) {
135→        if (!hasSelectedAnyRows()) {
136→            Timber.i("Not marking cards - nothing selected")
137→            return
138→        }
139→        undoableOp {
140→            val noteIds = notesOfCards(cardIds)
141→            // if all notes are marked, remove the mark
142→            // if no notes are marked, add the mark
143→            // if there is a mix, enable the mark on all
144→            val wantMark = !noteIds.all { getNote(it).hasTag("marked") }
145→            Timber.i("setting mark = %b for %d notes", wantMark, noteIds.size)
146→            if (wantMark) {
147→                tags.bulkAdd(noteIds, "marked")
148→            } else {
149→                tags.bulkRemove(noteIds, "marked")
150→            }
151→        }
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Deletes the selected notes,
156→     * @return the number of deleted notes
157→     */
158→    suspend fun deleteSelectedNotes(): Int =
159→        undoableOp { removeNotes(cids = selectedCardIds) }.count
160→
161→    fun setCardsOrNotes(newValue: CardsOrNotes) = viewModelScope.launch {
162→        withCol {
163→            // Change this to only change the preference on a state change
164→            newValue.saveToCollection(this)
165→        }
166→        cardsOrNotesFlow.update { newValue }
167→    }
168→
169→    fun setTruncated(value: Boolean) {
170→        viewModelScope.launch {
171→            isTruncatedFlow.emit(value)
172→        }
173→        sharedPrefs().edit {
174→            putBoolean("isTruncated", value)
175→        }
176→    }
177→
178→    fun selectAll() {
179→        if (_selectedRows.addAll(cards.wrapped)) {
180→            refreshSelectedRowsFlow()
181→        }
182→    }
183→
184→    fun selectNone() {
185→        if (_selectedRows.isEmpty()) return
186→        _selectedRows.clear()
187→        refreshSelectedRowsFlow()
188→    }
189→
190→    fun toggleRowSelectionAtPosition(position: Int) {
191→        val card = cards[position]
192→        if (_selectedRows.contains(card)) {
193→            _selectedRows.remove(card)
194→        } else {
195→            _selectedRows.add(card)
196→        }
197→        refreshSelectedRowsFlow()
198→    }
199→
200→    fun selectRowAtPosition(pos: Int) {
201→        if (_selectedRows.add(cards[pos])) {
202→            refreshSelectedRowsFlow()
203→        }
204→    }
205→
206→    /**
207→     * Selects the cards between [startPos] and [endPos]
208→     */
209→    fun selectRowsBetweenPositions(startPos: Int, endPos: Int) {
210→        val cards = (min(startPos, endPos)..max(startPos, endPos)).map { cards[it] }
211→        if (_selectedRows.addAll(cards)) {
212→            refreshSelectedRowsFlow()
213→        }
214→    }
215→
216→    /** emits a new value in [selectedRowsFlow] */
217→    private fun refreshSelectedRowsFlow() = viewModelScope.launch {
218→        refreshSelectedRowsFlow.emit(Unit)
219→    }
220→
221→    fun selectedRowCount(): Int = selectedRows.size
222→
223→    fun setColumn1Index(value: Int) = column1IndexFlow.update { value }
224→
225→    fun setColumn2Index(value: Int) = column2IndexFlow.update { value }
226→    suspend fun suspendCards() {
227→        val cardIds = selectedCardIds
228→        if (cardIds.isEmpty()) {
229→            return
230→        }
231→
232→        undoableOp {
233→            // if all cards are suspended, unsuspend all
234→            // if no cards are suspended, suspend all
235→            // if there is a mix, suspend all
236→            val wantUnsuspend = cardIds.all { getCard(it).queue == Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_SUSPENDED }
237→            if (wantUnsuspend) {
238→                sched.unsuspendCards(cardIds)
239→            } else {
240→                sched.suspendCards(cardIds).changes
241→            }
242→        }
243→    }
244→
245→    /** @return an [ExportDialogParams] based on the current screen state */
246→    suspend fun getExportDialogParams(resources: Resources): ExportDialogParams? {
247→        if (!isInMultiSelectMode) return null
248→        return when (cardsOrNotes) {
249→            CARDS -> {
250→                val selectedCardIds = selectedCardIds
251→                ExportDialogParams(
252→                    message = resources.getQuantityString(
253→                        R.plurals.confirm_apkg_export_selected_cards,
254→                        selectedCardIds.size,
255→                        selectedCardIds.size
256→                    ),
257→                    exportType = ExportType.ExportCards(selectedCardIds)
258→                )
259→            }
260→            NOTES -> {
261→                val selectedNoteIds = withCol { CardService.selectedNoteIds(selectedCardIds, this) }
262→                ExportDialogParams(
263→                    message = resources.getQuantityString(
264→                        R.plurals.confirm_apkg_export_selected_notes,
265→                        selectedNoteIds.size,
266→                        selectedNoteIds.size
267→                    ),
268→                    exportType = ExportType.ExportNotes(selectedNoteIds)
269→                )
270→            }
271→        }
272→    }
273→
274→    /**
275→     * @see [com.ichi2.libanki.sched.Scheduler.sortCards]
276→     * @return the number of cards which were repositioned
277→     */
278→    suspend fun repositionSelectedRows(position: Int) = undoableOp {
279→        sched.sortCards(selectedCardIds, position, 1, shuffle = false, shift = true)
280→    }.count
281→
282→    /** Returns the number of rows of the current result set  */
283→    val rowCount: Int

conversationHistory.content:
Perfect! I've identified the bug. Let me analyze the issue:

## Root Cause Analysis

Looking at the code in `CardBrowser.kt`:

**In `OnItemClickListener` (line 542-554):**
```kotlin
cardsListView.setOnItemClickListener { _, view, position, _ ->
    if (isInMultiSelectMode) {
        // click on whole cell triggers select
        val cb = view!!.findViewById<CheckBox>(R.id.card_checkbox)
        cb.toggle()  // Toggles the checkbox visual state
        viewModel.selectRowAtPosition(position)  // ONLY adds, never removes
    }
}
```

**In `OnItemLongClickListener` (line 556-572):**
```kotlin
cardsListView.setOnItemLongClickListener { _, view, position, _ ->
    if (!isInMultiSelectMode) {
        loadMultiSelectMode()
        val cb = view!!.findViewById<CheckBox>(R.id.card_checkbox)
        cb.toggle()
        viewModel.toggleRowSelectionAtPosition(position)  // Properly toggles (adds or removes)
    }
}
```

**The Bug:**
- When clicking a row during multi-select mode, `cb.toggle()` toggles the checkbox's visual state (e.g., checked → unchecked)
- But `selectRowAtPosition(position)` ONLY adds the card to the selected set, never removes it
- This causes the checkbox visual state and the actual selected state to become desynchronized
- When the view is rebound (e.g., after scrolling), `bindView()` sets `checkBox.isChecked = viewModel.selectedRows.contains(card)`, which shows the card as checked even though the checkbox was visually unchecked

**The Fix:**
Replace `selectRowAtPosition(position)` with `toggleRowSelectionAtPosition(position)` in the click handler to properly toggle the selection state.

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.174 (commit: b6ed71fd).

## Issue to Resolve

Feature Request: Implement 'User Action' Functionality Similar to AnkiMobile
In AnkiMobile (starting from version 2.0.88+), there is a feature that allows users to trigger JavaScript in the review screen. This advanced functionality is especially beneficial for those using shared decks that provide keyboard shortcuts to perform actions, such as revealing a hint or the next cloze deletion. By defining a user action, it's possible to trigger these actions with external devices, like a gamepad, or even by using gestures on the screen.

For instance, in AnkiMobile, if a card template provides a revealNext() function, users can instruct AnkiMobile to call it with the following code:

```
var userJs1 = revealNext;
```

This feature is especially handy for those who have written their own actions or are using certain shared decks/templates that provide these actions.

For more details, please refer to the [AnkiMobile documentation](https://docs.ankimobile.net/study-tools.html). Once on the page, scroll down to the "User Action 1-8" section for specifics.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing

 279→    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 280→        val extras = intent.extras
 281→        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 282→            // deckId is not set, load default
 283→            return
 284→        }
 285→        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 286→        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 287→
 288→        // deckId does not exist, load default
 289→        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 290→            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 291→            return
 292→        }
 293→        // Select the deck
 294→        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 295→    }

 309→    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 310→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 311→        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 312→            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 313→            selectDeckFromExtra()
 314→        }
 315→        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 316→        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 317→        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 318→        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 319→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 320→            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 321→            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 322→            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 323→            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 324→            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 325→            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 326→        }
 327→        launchCatchingTask {
 328→            withCol { startTimebox() }
 329→            updateCardAndRedraw()
 330→        }
 331→        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 332→
 333→        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 334→        if (prefFullscreenReview) {
 335→            setFullScreen(this)
 336→        }
 337→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 338→    }

 564→    override fun closeReviewer(result: Int) {

 590→    private fun openOrToggleMicToolbar() {
 591→        if (!canRecordAudio(this)) {
 592→            ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(
 593→                this,
 594→                arrayOf(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO),
 595→                REQUEST_AUDIO_PERMISSION
 596→            )
 597→        } else {
 598→            toggleMicToolBar()
 599→        }
 600→    }
 601→
 602→    private fun toggleMicToolBar() {
 603→        tempAudioPath = generateTempAudioFile(this)
 604→        if (isMicToolBarVisible) {
 605→            micToolBarLayer.visibility = View.GONE
 606→        } else {
 607→            setReviewerStatus(false)
 608→            if (!isAudioUIInitialized) {
 609→                try {
 610→                    audioRecordingController = AudioRecordingController()
 611→                    audioRecordingController?.createUI(this, micToolBarLayer)
 612→                } catch (e: Exception) {
 613→                    Timber.w(e, "unable to add the audio recorder to toolbar")
 614→                    CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport(e, "Unable to create recorder tool bar")
 615→                    showThemedToast(
 616→                        this,
 617→                        this.getText(R.string.multimedia_editor_audio_view_create_failed).toString(),
 618→                        true
 619→                    )
 620→                }
 621→                isAudioUIInitialized = true
 622→            }
 623→            micToolBarLayer.visibility = View.VISIBLE
 624→        }
 625→        isMicToolBarVisible = !isMicToolBarVisible
 626→    }

 628→    override fun onRequestPermissionsResult(requestCode: Int, permissions: Array<String>, grantResults: IntArray) {
 629→        super.onRequestPermissionsResult(requestCode, permissions, grantResults)
 630→        if (requestCode == REQUEST_AUDIO_PERMISSION &&
 631→            permissions.isNotEmpty() && grantResults[0] == PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED
 632→        ) {
 633→            // Get get audio record permission, so we can create the record tool bar
 634→            toggleMicToolBar()
 635→        }
 636→    }

 972→    private fun updateScreenCounts() {
 973→        val queue = queueState ?: return
 974→        super.updateActionBar()
 975→        val actionBar = supportActionBar
 976→        val counts = queue.counts
 977→        if (actionBar != null) {
 978→            if (prefShowETA) {
 979→                launchCatchingTask {
 980→                    eta = withCol { sched.eta(counts, false) }
 981→                    actionBar.subtitle = remainingTime(this@Reviewer, (eta * 60).toLong())
 982→                }
 983→            }
 984→        }
 985→        newCount = SpannableString(counts.new.toString())
 986→        lrnCount = SpannableString(counts.lrn.toString())
 987→        revCount = SpannableString(counts.rev.toString())
 988→        if (prefHideDueCount) {
 989→            revCount = SpannableString("???")
 990→        }
 991→        // if this code is run as a card is being answered, currentCard may be non-null but
 992→        // the queues may be empty - we can't call countIdx() in such a case
 993→        if (counts.count() != 0) {
 994→            when (queue.countsIndex) {
 995→                Counts.Queue.NEW -> newCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, newCount!!.length, 0)
 996→                Counts.Queue.LRN -> lrnCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, lrnCount!!.length, 0)
 997→                Counts.Queue.REV -> revCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, revCount!!.length, 0)
 998→            }
 999→        }
1000→        textBarNew.text = newCount
1001→        textBarLearn.text = lrnCount
1002→        textBarReview.text = revCount
1003→    }

1032→    override suspend fun answerCardInner(ease: Int) {
1033→        val state = queueState!!
1034→        Timber.d("answerCardInner: ${currentCard!!.id} $ease")
1035→        var wasLeech = false
1036→        undoableOp(this) {
1037→            sched.answerCard(state, ease).also {
1038→                wasLeech = sched.againIsLeech(state)
1039→            }
1040→        }.also {
1041→            if (ease == Consts.BUTTON_ONE && wasLeech) {
1042→                state.topCard.load(getColUnsafe)
1043→                val leechMessage: String = if (state.topCard.queue < 0) {
1044→                    resources.getString(R.string.leech_suspend_notification)
1045→                } else {
1046→                    resources.getString(R.string.leech_notification)
1047→                }
1048→                showSnackbar(leechMessage, Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
1049→            }
1050→        }
1051→    }

1053→    private fun dealWithTimeBox(timebox: Collection.TimeboxReached) {
1054→        val nCards = timebox.reps
1055→        val nMins = timebox.secs / 60
1056→        val mins = resources.getQuantityString(R.plurals.in_minutes, nMins, nMins)
1057→        val timeboxMessage = resources.getQuantityString(R.plurals.timebox_reached, nCards, nCards, mins)
1058→        AlertDialog.Builder(this).show {
1059→            title(R.string.timebox_reached_title)
1060→            message(text = timeboxMessage)
1061→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_continue) {}
1062→            negativeButton(text = CollectionManager.TR.studyingFinish()) {
1063→                finish()
1064→            }
1065→            cancelable(true)
1066→            setOnCancelListener { }
1067→        }
1068→    }

1095→    private fun runStateMutationHook() {
1096→        val state = queueState ?: return
1097→        if (state.customSchedulingJs.isEmpty()) {
1098→            statesMutated = true
1099→            return
1100→        }
1101→        val key = customSchedulingKey
1102→        val js = state.customSchedulingJs
1103→        webView?.evaluateJavascript(
1104→            """
1105→        anki.mutateNextCardStates('$key', async (states, customData, ctx) => { $js })
1106→            .catch(err => { console.log(err); window.location.href = "state-mutation-error:"; });
1107→"""
1108→        ) { result ->
1109→            if ("null" == result) {
1110→                // eval failed, usually a syntax error
1111→                // Note, we get "null" (string) and not null
1112→                statesMutated = true
1113→            }
1114→        }
1115→    }
